INCIDENTTITLE: Select at MINES site at 26 Aug 2025, 11:26 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 26th August 2025, it was discovered that the Fiber cable located at the backside area of the ETP in Kalarangi Mines had been cut. Due to this damage, five (05) numbers of CCTV cameras went down from 25th August 2025 at 16:10 hrs.
The information regarding the cable cut was communicated by the CCTV maintenance team (Vita Team – Mr. P. C. Khilar) during their site inspection. Preliminary findings suggest that the Fiber wire was intentionally cut with the objective of theft or sabotage.
Informed to vita communication after down the CCTV then, Vita Team Site Eng. reached at site, then he was inspecting the area and found cable was cut backside of ETP area.
And resolved the issue then camera was live.

INCIDENTTITLE: Property Related at BALCO site at 2025-07-23 23:51
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 23rd July 2025 at around 23:51 hours, Security Personnel, deployed at the In checking post, observed suspicious movement involving 3–4 unidentified individuals near the plant boundary wall. He immediately reported the incident to Plant-02 Shift In-charge. Plant-02 Shift In-charge promptly informed the Central Security Operations Center (CSOC) about the miscreants' movement. In response, the CSOC Shift In-charge dispatched the Quick Response Team (QRT) for additional support. Acting swiftly, Shift In-charge, accompanied by PS, pursued the suspects in a vehicle from outside the premises. During the chase, one of the miscreants, identified as G. Sonu, son of G. Simon, was apprehended by the Shift In-charge. Upon inspection, four focus lights with value of Rs.- 4000/- were recovered from outside the boundary wall near the main road. The team immediately contacted the 112 police helpline, and upon arrival, the police personnel took custody of the apprehended individual for further legal action. The case has been registered under section 109 at Police Station Balco Nagar.

INCIDENTTITLE: Offsite Incidents at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 16 Oct 2025, 7:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
As per the information received, an iron ore-loaded tipper (Vedanta)bearing registration number KA24 4056 collided with a motorcycle KA16VZ 1295. The incident resulted in the death of the bike rider, Mr. Manoj, on the spot. The pillion rider, Mr. Ashok, sustained injuries and was evacuated from the site for medical attention (by vedanta ambulance).
Immediately matter informed to Sirigere Police station and Chikjajur Police station. Police team carried out panchnama and dead body evacuated from the site for Postmortem. Bike and tipper vehicles taken in Police Custody. At around 15:15Hrs dead body brought to village after completion of postmortem and at around cremation was completed.


Vehicle & Personnel Details:
Tipper:
•	Registration No.: KA24 4056
•	Driver: Pradeep, Resident of Kagalgere Village
•	Owner: Bhasvaraj, Resident of Kagalgere Village
•	Status: Loaded tipper exited from Vedanta Mines
Motorcycle:
•	Registration No.: KA16VZ 1295
•	Rider: Ashok, Resident of Kagalgere Village (Injured)
•	Pillion Rider: Manoj, Resident of Kagalgere Village (Deceased)


Response and Action:- 

1.	Matter informed to Sirigere and Chikjajur Police station.
2.	Diverted QRT vehicle at the incident site.
3.	Police team carried out Panchana and dead body send for Postmortem.
4.	Accident bike and tipper vehicle taken in Police custody.


Police Concerns and Recommendations:- 

During a discussion, the Holalkere CI raised several concerns and recommendations for immediate corrective action: 

* Road Repair: Proper maintenance and repair of the road. 

* Safety Measures: Installation of signboards and speed breakers on the road. 

* Speed Control: Mandatory fitting of speed-locking mechanisms on all vehicles. 

* Vehicle Information: Sharing of all Tipper vehicle details with the Police station.

INCIDENTTITLE: Perimeter wall collapse due to excessive rainfall at SILVASSA site on 19 Jun 2025, 12:40 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 19th June 2025 at approx. 1240hrs, Traffic Marshall Anshu Singh reported via radio that the perimeter wall between BW no 69 and 70 had collapsed. Upon receiving the message, Security SIC Sweta Kumari and Head Security Heramb Joshi promptly arrived at the incident site. It was determined that the wall had been obscured by bushes for an extended period, and its base had been further weakened due to excessive rainfall in Silvassa. Head Security passed information to Head Civil Department AV Durgaprasad, Head External Affairs Kiran Joshi, and IBU Director Sathiya Narayanmoorthy. To address the situation promptly, it was decided to install GI sheets and Civil Engineer Sankalp Deshmukh was assigned the responsibility.

INCIDENTTITLE: Property Related at BALCO site at 10 Jun 2025, 9:43 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 10.06.2025 at approximately 2143 hrs, security personnel reported an attempted theft of aluminium plates near the IPPL Store in the Plant 1 Area, where the suspects fled upon being noticed. An alert was immediately communicated to the Plant 1 Shift In-Charge, the nearest deployed guard, and the Quick Response Team (QRT). Following the alert, the security team promptly arrived at the location and conducted a thorough search operation both inside and outside the boundary. At around 2203 hrs, QRT member SG Sanjay Patley observed suspicious behaviour from a worker exiting through the Alumina 2W Out Gate, who escaped when approached. Upon noticing the security presence, the miscreants abandoned the aluminium scrap they were attempting to steal and fled the scene. CCTV footage identified the suspect as Mr. Mukesh Kumar (ID: 1604528), an employee of M/s Lizmontagens India. The team recovered 53 kgs of aluminium scrap, which has been stored securely. The matter has been escalated to the HR department, and the gate pass of the identified individual has been blocked pending further investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at CLZS(CHANDERIYA) site at 2025-06-17 15:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 17th June 2025, at approximately 1040 hrs, a missing person report was received from the Sarpanch of Ajoliya ka Kheda village regarding Mr. Prabhu Lal Teli, a septuagenarian (approx. 75 years old), who had gone missing while grazing animals near the Nallah flowing alongside the plant premises.
Based on this input, the Quick Response Team (QRT) was deployed, and a coordinated search operation was conducted along the plant boundary.
At around 1345 hrs, information was received regarding a body floating in the Nallah. A joint rescue operation involving Security, Safety, and Police teams was carried out. By 1530 hrs, the body was recovered and transported via ambulance to the government hospital for post-mortem, under police supervision.
1553 hrs: Approximately 30–40 villagers gathered at the CLZS Main Gate. A discussion was initiated with 4–5 representatives, but failed to reach resolution. The villagers subsequently blocked the entry gate. Police and Tehsildar arrived at the scene. No Business interruption happened.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2025-06-23 12:15
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On dated 23.06.2025 at around 08:15Hrs, information received from sources that Mr. Sidhana along with some 30 nos of tipper drivers may come JSW Y Junction to block the Y Junction road for the demand to start the JSW Iron ore transportation movement from Vedanta mines internal road to Saslu Rly Siding. Latter information received that around 100 plus local peoples may also join to Mr. Sidhana.
Information shared with all concerned persons and preventative measure taken like working of CCTV Camera at location, Block the mentioned location road by placing the two trucks, Locked the gate with 04 lock & chain, Deployed the bouncers (Special guard), Informed shared with Rural police station, informed HR department for arranging the Company Workmen & Business partner staff at location.
At around 12:15Hrs, peoples started gathering and around 150 plus persons gathered outside the Y Junction gate. Main leaders available at the location was Mr. Manjana (Ex Counsler chitradurga Brurujanahatti ), Mr. Prakash (Chitradurga), Mr.Tipeswamy (from Brurujanahatti) and other public from locations of Hunesekatte, Siddavvanahalli, Hirehuntur, Midekepura and Brurujanahatti. 
From Vedanta side Mr. Rohit Kumar (CSO), Mr. Kiran Kumar (DyCSO), Mr. Pothilingam (CHRO), Mr. Vijay Metha (Head Logistic), Mr. Ravi Bisaguppi (Head CSR), Mr. Ravi Nayaka (ER/PR), Mr. Shrinivas Pattar (Rly Logistic), MR. Umapathi  (Union),  Security Manpower, Bouncer, Workmen and BP of almost 150 Plus was available at the location.
At around 13:10Hrs, Vedanta team discussed the matter with the gathered persons and they were demanding to open the Y Junction Gate for the JSW Saslu route movement and very aggressive during the discussion.
At around 13:25Hrs Police staff reached at location and pacify the gathering.
At around 14:35Hrs, Circle Inspector reached at the location and talked to gathered public, Vedanta and JSW team however no solution and CI suggested to have the joint meeting in Dy SP  office for further discussion but these peoples were not agree for the same.
At around 16:00Hrs, Dy SP Chitradurga and Dy RFO reached at the location and discussed the matter however public not agree and they were continuously demanding to open the gate to start the JSW movement from Vedanta internal roads.
At around 16:30Hrs, during discussion some peoples in gathered public were in drunken condition and  tried to manhandle the Vedanta staff however available Police team and Security staff stopped the same.
At around 16:55Hrs, some people from gathering broken the Y Junction Gate lock with hammer and opened the gate. Vedanta team is available at location. Latter Police and Forest team asked Vedanta team to remove the trucks from the location.
At around 18:50Hrs, After discussion it was decided to had meeting in DCF office dated 24.06.2025 and public started dispersing from the location.

Latest Update:- 
On dated 23.06.2025, following news channel visited to the location and captures the details.
1.	TV9
2.	Suvarna news
3.	Public TV
On 24.06.2025 at around 11:10 hrs, Approx 12 nos of drivers gathered at JSW Y junction and asked why Vedanta team put the lock on gate and returned at 1235 hrs.
At around 15:48Hrs, ACF, DyRFO, other forest officers and Police 03 constables, JSW Mines Manager, Mr. Shrinivas Pattar from Vedanta Mines, Mr.Siddhana, Mr.Manjana, Mr.Prahald from local peoples available at Y Junction. Following points discuss with ACF and following points cleared from Vedanta side and accepted by JSW Mines Manager -
1.	Alcohol Check and DL to be compulsory checked for Vehicles drivers

2.	Speed Limit to be followed by Trucks
3.	Only single driver will be allowed with each truck.

Total 06 vehicles released from Y Junction towards Gate 2 and reached Gate 1 via mines road and exit from Gate 1 at around 17:10Hrs. After crossing the Gate 1, all JSW trucks hold by Gate 1 tipper drivers. 
Police Compliant related 23rd June 2025 incident given to SP, DySP and Rural Police Station.
Siddhana hold the approx. 30 nos of tipper vehicles in Mudhukur village related to Johan Mines for the demand of release of JSW trucks outside Gate 1.
Shyamana was installed in Mudukur village.

On 25.06.2025 at around 1330 hrs, Chikkajajur police team visited the location and guided association people to file police complaint to stop tipper movement by siddanna at muthugadur village and association members went to DC and SP office to file a police complaint against JSW Sasulu movement. 
Polce complaint also lodged by Vedanta against JSW sasulu movement and stoppage of tippers at gate #1 route at Chikkajajur police station. 
On 26.06.2025 at around 1320 hrs, based on police complaint by Vedanta, police team called JSW & Vedanta Representatives at Chikkajajur police station for discussion. A meeting held at seregire mutt in which transportation, association and siddanna available at the location. 
Post discussion, Tipper vehicles were released at 1545 hrs. Chikkajaur police reached at gate #1 to take JSW sasulu trucks inside from gate #1and via gate #2 and Y-Junction at 1750 hrs.05 no. of JSW trucks exited from Y-Junction and moved towards JSW at 1835 hrs. Also 6th JSW truck exited from Y-Junction at around 2025 hrs.
 On 27.06.2025 at around 0605 hrs, around 40 no. of persons gathered at medikepura to stop the tipper movement by demand of their new tipper vehicles to ply on gate #1 route and installed shamiyana at medikepura security post. Seregire police reached at the location of shamiyana for discussion. 
Post joint discussion at around 1610 hrs with john mines team, tipper vehicle movement started from medikepura and decided for induction of new vehicles at gate #1 route on Monday in seregire mutt. Installed shamiyana removed at 1815 hrs. 
On 28.06.2025 at around 0935 hrs, Siddanna diverted 14 no. of tippers from sasulu Flyover towards JSW via B. Durga.14 tippers vehicles crossed Y-unction and moved towards JSW mines at 1015 hrs. Siddanna and Manjanna (Ex. Counseller) went to JSW at 1110 hrs.15 no. of tippers were loaded by JSW for Sasulu movement at 1145 hrs. Loaded JSW sasulu movement and came to Y-Junction at around 1250 hrs. Vedanta team closed the Y-Junction gate and blocked the road by placing the trucks. Post discussion ,opened Y-Junction gate and released Vedanta trucks and then released the tipper vehicles at 1410 hrs.
As Vedanta team closed gate #2 so 34 Vedanta trucks and 14 no.of tippers were at outside of gate #2 at 1415 hrs. Siddanna collected the DMG tappal of all tipper vehicles and went back to JSW along with all tipper vehicle drivers at 1425 hrs. 
At around 1630 hrs, Dy.RFO Raghavender ,Mr. Vinod (JSW Mines manager) and one junior forest officer came to Admin block to give one latter and exited at 1700 hrs. All Vedanta trucks outside gate # 2 was taken inside and kept one wheel loader at 1640 hrs and cleared one way road outside gate #2. At round 1700 hrs, siddanna and tipper drivers exited from Y-Junction towards king gate. At around 1920 hrs ,02 forest guards came to Y-Junction, and they went towards gate #2 to take pictures of hold tipper vehicles. 
Siddanta along with 14 persons reached at Y-junction and they waited for RFO.All drivers reached at gate #2 at 2050 hrs.15 tippers taken inside from gate #2 and moved towards gate #1 with security escort. All 14 tippers were exited gate #1 at 20:55 hrs. All tipper vehicles were hold at john mines junction at 21:05 due to road blockage by placing empty tipper vehicles on road by landowners from tanegehalli cross to medekepura due to land settlement. 
On 29.06.2025 at around 1300 hrs, loaded tippers were released from location and next loading of tipper would happen based on discussion and all stakeholders must be aligned.

INCIDENTTITLE: Property Related at BALCO site at 05 Jul 2025, 5:25 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On July 5th, 2025, at approximately 17:25 hrs, Mr. Amrit Lal Bhardwaj (UID: 80000012949), an employee of KEC International Limited, was stopped during routine security frisking at the Parsabhata pedestrian exit gate. A company-owned digging tool (Kudali) worth Rs. 100/-, taken from the GAP site, was found in his bag. During questioning by Zone 3 In-Charge, Mr. Bhardwaj admitted to attempting to take the tool out of the premises without proper authorization. His supervisor, Mr. Anil Kumar Aacharya, was also called in for further inquiry. Statements from both individuals were recorded, and Mr. Bhardwaj submitted a written statement, accepting responsibility for the act. His gate pass was confiscated, and the matter has been escalated to the concerned HR department for necessary action.

INCIDENTTITLE: Property Related at BALCO site at 30 Jun 2025, 2:35 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2:35 AM, the CSOC team observed suspicious activity via CCTV involving 4–5 individuals near the SPL Shed WT 09 boundary wall in Plant 2. They promptly alerted the shift in-charges of Plant 1 and 2, along with both internal and external Quick Response Teams (QRT).
An initial search by the security team yielded no results. However, the QRT later organized an ambush. The suspects, dressed like workmen in full PPE, were difficult to distinguish from authorized personnel. When they attempted to flee by scaling the boundary wall, the QRT gave chase. In response, the suspects began hurling stones at the security personnel.
During the pursuit, the suspects abandoned several items near the boundary wall and escaped. Upon thorough inspection, five sacks containing aluminium were recovered. The total weight was 140 kilograms, valued at approximately ?42,000.A comprehensive investigation is currently underway.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 09 Dec 2025, 11:06 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 2306hrs, a call was received from Katikela Ash Pond Supervisor regarding the presence of miscreants and cutting of the DG Power Cable. Security Guard Sunil Bagh observed one miscreant who was engaged in cutting the cable and immediately informed  Plant-1 Duty Officer. Baaz Team, Plant-1 QRT, and SEZ/ TPP  QRT reached the location and conducted a search, but no one was found. Approximately 20 meters of power cable and two hexa blades were recovered from the spot.
RCA-Lack of effective surveillance and patrolling at the Katikela Ash Pond enabled miscreants to access the area and attempt theft by cutting the DG power cable.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 07 Apr 2025, 7:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 1950hrs, a call was received from the Katikela Ash Pond supervisor informing that a group of 10-12 miscreants had arrived at Pipeline No. 1, armed with lathis and knives. They threatened the on-duty guard by placing a knife to his neck, verbally abused him, and stole 04 small pieces of pipe from the location. The information was immediately relayed to the CCR. At around 2010hrs, the QRT and Baaz team responded to the spot; however, the miscreants had already fled the scene by the time the team arrived. Team Searched the whole area near the river site but no material or miscreants movement observed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 07 Apr 2025, 1:27 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 0127, SSG Santosh Barik, informed that miscreant movement at Cooling tower 9 (K11) area. QRTs responded and immediately reached the location and thoroughly searched the area and found a cut on PIDS fiber cable,  recovered 120mtr MOV(Motor Operated Valve) wire from inside the plant and two fox holes. Due to the timely response of QRTs and vigilance of guard miscreants were fled away from the location without material. The foxholes were temporarily closed with GI sheet.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at VAL J site at 02 Apr 2025, 5:15 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 1715hrs, during the exit check of an RGP material (gearbox part) from the Potline Service department, we identified a discrepancy in RGP-PLANT-1 20250402-11411 (cost mentioned as ?2 lakhs) related to service maintenance. According to the documents, the material arrived on August 2, 2024, indicating a duration of only seven months, which is unusually short for requiring maintenance. The material was sent for repair under PO 6600016643 (M/S Janata Engineering). The vehicle was held at Fortune Gate FG parking for verification. After receiving a reply via email from the Potline Service InCharge, Mr. Akhila Ranjan Sahoo, stating that the actual material sent for service was an old spare part and clarifying the need for regeneration of the RGP as per the actual GRN, the RGP material was sent back to the user department and subsequently cancelled.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at VAL J site at 03 Apr 2025, 5:24 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 1724hrs, CPP security supervisor reported about some miscreants attempted to make a foxhole at the CPP scrap yard. Upon spotting the guard, they fled the scene. The information was immediately relayed to the CCR and Baaz team and the QRT responded to the location. A thorough search of the area was conducted, but nothing was found/recovered. However, during the search, it was discovered that the fiber cable of the PIDS project had been cut at two points near K-11, between pole numbers 30 and 32. The guard was instructed to maintain heightened vigilance in the area. At around 1830hrs, the foxhole was temporarily closed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 03 Dec 2025, 10:27 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 2227 hrs. During regular patrolling one of our KEC guards, Ashish Bhainsal, informed us that 2 intruders were spotted inside the premises and one on the boundary wall. The SEZ QRT (Quick Response Team) quickly responded to the area and simultaneously informed the control room. Due to the guard's alertness, the intruders fled the scene. However, during the search operation conducted by the SEZ team, 3 iron rods and 1 ladder were recovered. Further search operations were carried out in collaboration with the BAAZ team and TPP, resulting in the recovery of an additional 20 pieces of iron rod, 2 lights, and 2 pieces of wire from the outside of plant premises.
RCA-Perimeter and material security gaps (weak deterrence/monitoring near boundary and unsecured scrap/materials), enabling intruder access and attempted theft.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 07 Apr 2025, 2:24 AM

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at VAL J site at 07 Dec 2025, 4:08 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 0408 to 0529 hrs. Local community from Banjari, Kurebagha, Tharkimal & Katikela staged protests at plant gates and Siriyapali ash pond. Issues raised included illumination, road construction, temple renovation, employment demands, and land/NOC concerns. To ensure business continuity, movement was re-routed through emergency gate to the plant.  Gate Management team including External , CSR and Security worked on resolution with the protestors and as precautionary measure police mobilized at site. Major crew changes were completed in early hours based on inputs received .  There was a minor disruption faced during this period. By 0815 hrs. movement through all gates resumed following discussions with internal teams and police authorities and smooth operations ensured. Work on engagement and permanent resolution  is in progress.
RCA-Community protests occurred due to unresolved local issues like poor illumination, pending road works, temple renovation, employment demands, and land/NOC concerns, combined with inadequate proactive engagement and communication.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at IRON AND POWER site at 2025-05-09 19:00
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"At around 1900hrs, QRT-1  informed Security Control Room that a Hywa collided with boom barrier near BF-2 due to which the boom barrier broke.
 Vehicle details:
Vehicle No -JH10CQ 6597
 Driver-MD Daud

The traffic team immediately reached on the spot and conducted alcohol test, which was found negative. INCIDENTTITLE: Strike at Main Gate
INCIDENTDETAILS: Villagers of Bundel were on strike at Plant main gate in the protest of unemployment and pending welfare works for the village.

INCIDENTTITLE: Strike at Main Gate
INCIDENTDETAILS: Residents of NVN colony were on strike at main gate and material gate over unresolved job related issues, specifically concerning employment for widows of former employees.

INCIDENTTITLE: Foiled attempt at Baliguda tower area.
INCIDENTDETAILS: 330 kgs of raw copper of Transformer were being taken away from the location by the miscreants.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 26 May 2025, 12:22 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 0022 hrs, CCTV operators observed that some miscreants had entered the boundary of Garga Yard-1 with the intention of theft. Upon receiving the information, the Shift IC and QRT-4 promptly proceeded to the location. When the security team arrived on the spot, the miscreants began pelting stones at them. In retaliation, the security personnel took defensive measures, which caused the miscreants to flee and hide in the nearby bushes. Despite continued stone-pelting, through persistent efforts by the security team and the Shift IC, the miscreants eventually fled the area by 0230 hrs. No theft was reported from the yard following the incident.

INCIDENTTITLE: Railway Strike at Chandanpur village
INCIDENTDETAILS: A railway strike was commenced at Chandanpur due to a caustic rake colliding with a cow approximately 1 KM ahead of Mid Station, en route to the plant.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft at CHP Control Room
INCIDENTDETAILS: Aprox. 20 miscreants entered at CHP Control room having traditional weapons and took away a bundle of copper cable from the location.

INCIDENTTITLE: Strike at Main Gate
INCIDENTDETAILS: Local resedents of nearby villages were on strike at Plant Main Gate due to unemployment and salary issue.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at STEEL site at 2025-05-20 00:53
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 00:53 hrs, the WRM Shift In-Charge, Mr. Amit Kumar Gupta, received information that a crane operator, Low Kumar Chaudhary (Employee ID: 110839), employed by Chavi Infra Engineering Private Limited and working in the WRM area, was under the influence of alcohol. The security team immediately proceeded to the location and conducted an alcohol test, which confirmed a positive result with a Blood Alcohol Content (BAC) level of 2.76%. This incident was officially reported via email to the concerned department. The individual was escorted out and sent back home through Karma Gate.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 24 May 2025, 10:05 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 2205 hrs, CCTV operators observed some miscreants entering the boundary of Garga Yard-2 with the apparent intention of committing theft. The CRO immediately informed QRT-4 and the Shift IC. Upon receiving the information, the Shift IC and QRT-4 promptly proceeded to the location. When the security team arrived on the spot, the miscreants began pelting stones at them. In retaliation, the security personnel took defensive measures, which caused the miscreants to flee and hide in the nearby bushes. Despite continued stone-pelting, through persistent efforts by the security team and the Shift IC, the miscreants eventually fled the area by 0130 hrs and 0030 hrs. No theft was reported from the yard following the incident.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at CLZS(CHANDERIYA) site at 01 Jun 2025, 4:00 PM

"It has come to notice that Narayan Mali, of fumer plant has been carrying the gate pass of Deepak Saini, Fumer plant over the past few days. This gate pass permits the carrying of a mobile phone, which Narayan has been utilizing.
While the gate pass allows for mobile phone usage, it is imperative to note that such permissions are typically intended for specific individuals and purposes. The unauthorized use of another employee's gate pass raises concerns about adherence to security measures and the potential for misuse.
According to statement Narayan mali mentioned that this was given to him by Deepak Saini for bringing breakfast and after bringing breakfast Narayan mali forgot to handover the card to Deepak Saini.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at BALCO site at 24 Jun 2025, 2:05 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 24, 2025, at 1405 hrs, during a surprise security check at the Inbound Gate, security personnel apprehended Mr. Niraj Singh (License No.: UP5720210006686), a coal driver from Transporter RKTC, for possession of prohibited material. Mr. Singh was found carrying two bottles of alcohol concealed in his vehicle (Registration No.: CG12BS4240). The security personnel immediately reported to the Centralized Security Operations Centre (CSOC), the Plant 2 Shift In-Charge. CSOC immediately communicated to QRT Team, Plant 2 Incharge and all other relevant authorities. The Plant 2 Shift In-Charge and QR team promptly arrived at the site and conducted a preliminary investigation. The prohibited items were seized on the spot, and a written statement was taken from Mr. Singh. His license was confiscated, and the matter was formally communicated to the HR department for further action.

INCIDENTTITLE: On 20.12.2025 at approximately 0835 hrs, shift vehicles of  Davanagere

INCIDENTTITLE: Perimeter wall collapse due to excessive rainfall
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 19th June 2025 at approx. 1240hrs, Traffic Marshall Anshu Singh reported via radio that the perimeter

wall between BW no 69 and 70 had collapsed. Upon receiving the message, Security SIC Sweta Kumari

and Head Security Heramb Joshi promptly arrived at the incident site. It was determined that the wall had

been obscured by bushes for an extended period, and its base had been further weakened due to

excessive rainfall in Silvassa. Head Security passed information to Head Civil Department AV

Durgaprasad, Head External Affairs Kiran Joshi, and IBU Director Sathiya Narayanmoorthy. To address

the situation promptly, it was decided to install GI sheets and Civil Engineer Sankalp Deshmukh was

assigned the responsibility.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at BALCO site at 10 Jun 2025, 9:43 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 10.06.2025 at approximately 2143 hrs, security personnel reported an attempted theft of aluminium plates near the IPPL Store in the Plant 1 Area, where the suspects fled upon being noticed. An alert was immediately communicated to the Plant 1 Shift In-Charge, the nearest deployed guard, and the Quick Response Team (QRT). Following the alert, the security team promptly arrived at the location and conducted a thorough search operation both inside and outside the boundary. At around 2203 hrs, QRT member SG Sanjay Patley observed suspicious behaviour from a worker exiting through the Alumina 2W Out Gate, who escaped when approached. Upon noticing the security presence, the miscreants abandoned the aluminium plates they were attempting to steal and fled the scene. CCTV footage identified the suspect as Mr. Mukesh Kumar (ID: 1604528), an employee of M/s Lizmontagens India. The team recovered 53 kgs of aluminium plates, which has been stored securely. The matter has been escalated to the HR department, and the gate pass of the identified individual has been blocked pending further investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at BALCO site at 25 Jun 2025, 6:10 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 25, 2025, at 0610 hrs during Shift A, CSOC received an intelligence input regarding possible unauthorized material movement at the Parsabhata 2-wheeler out gate in Plant 3. Acting on the alert, CSOC immediately notified the security team to conduct thorough checking at the location. During frisking, Mr. Gagan Sharma (UID: 1701827), a subcontractor under M/s Sivasakthi Power Services, was found attempting to carry out two wires and one MCB Valued at 2,760 and 1,250 respectively hidden in the storage compartment of his Scooty (CG12BM6938). The incident was promptly reported to the Plant 3 Zonal Incharge and all concerned Person. Upon investigation, Mr. Sharma claimed the items were purchased for personal use and presented a bill as proof. The recovered materials were seized, his statement was recorded in the presence of his supervisor, Mr. Aman Verma (UID: 1701830), and his gate pass was confiscated. The matter has been escalated to the HR department for further action.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 01 Apr 2025, 2:31 AM

The police team was also informed of this incident.

INCIDENTTITLE: Chitradurga shift buses were stopped at Bheemasamudra village at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 24 Sep 2025, 5:25 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Chitradurga shift buses were stopped at Bheemasamudra village by Santosh Kumar and Kariyappa, who demanded jobs. After discussions between the security team and bouncers, the issue was temporarily resolved, and the shift vehicles were released at around 0545 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 18 Sep 2025, 4:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 1600 hrs, information was received regarding the movement of two suspected miscreants behind the Sepco store. As the QRT team reached the location, the individuals fled the area and jumped over the boundary wall near perimeter point 341.
During the subsequent search operation in the nearby jungle area, two bags containing scrap material were found. The recovered scrap was secured and placed behind the Security Office building

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 19 Sep 2025, 2:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 14:30 hrs, the Railway Track Security Guard informed SV Boota Singh about a miscreant stealing scrap from the railway track. The QRT team, along with the Security Officer (SO), promptly responded and reached the location within five minutes.
The team successfully apprehended the miscreant along with the stolen scrap. The individual was subsequently handed over to Baniwal Police Chonki for further action.
LNAME: Railway Control Room, Pond, Tower -8, CHP Control Room & workshop area

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 19 Sep 2025, 10:15 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2215 hrs, while on routine patrol, the QRT team observed suspicious activity near perimeter point 83. As the team initiated a search, three miscreants were seen fleeing towards the boundary wall and quickly escaped by jumping over it.
Upon inspection, it was found that the concertina coil at that location had been damaged. Additionally, some scrap material was discovered hidden in the nearby jungle area. The recovered scrap was secured and placed behind the Security Office building.
LNAME: Scrap yard, Raipur Railway gate, Tower -9, Central Store

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 20 Sep 2025, 7:15 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0715 hrs, Ptl-1 Supervisor Amrik Singh reported that while patrolling in the Zand area, he observed two miscreants attempting to throw scrap material over the boundary wall in the Tekri area.
Upon noticing the approaching security vehicle, the miscreants abandoned the scrap near the boundary wall and fled outside the premises. The scrap was subsequently recovered and secured at the backside of the Security Office building.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at CLZS(CHANDERIYA) site at 28 Sep 2025, 1:35 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 28th September 2025 at 13:35 hrs, Mr. Samirruddin Seikh(GP no.:CH60015496)  was observed by the control room operator to have punched in at the Turnstile and allowed Mr. Riajuddin Ahamed(GP no.: CH60016335) to enter the premises using his punch. Subsequently, Mr. Samirruddin himself entered after punching again. Both individuals were intercepted at the same time by security staff. (Video link:   28th Sept 2025(Afternoon).mp4)
Additionally, it was found out by punching report that Mr. Riajuddin Ahamed punched in at 07:59 hrs the same morning and when checked by cctv footage it was observed that Riajuddin Ahamed rotated the Turnstile without actually entering the premises. (Video link:   28th Sept 2025(Morning).mp4)

Upon further review and investigation , it was established that Mr. Samirruddin is habitual in this misconduct. Examination of CCTV footage with reference to punch records indicated that he regularly permitted others to enter the plant on his punch and subsequently exited along with those individuals after a short interval, using their respective punches.

INCIDENTTITLE: Mr. Satish from V Palya Village obstructed the Y-Junction near Gate #2  as on 26 Sep 2025, 7:45 AM.
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 26th September 2025, at approximately 07:45 hrs, Mr. Satish from V Palya Village obstructed the Y-Junction near Gate #2, which serves as a critical connection between our mine and the BBH Railway Siding. He placed two water tankers at the location, disrupting mining operations. Following discussions, the tankers were removed around 13:00 hrs.
However, at approximately 14:15 hrs, Mr. Satish again blocked the same road using the same two water tankers, claiming possession of an injunction order for the land and stating that Vedanta vehicles were not permitted to use the road. This action resulted in a complete blockade of the route to the BBH Railway Siding, severely impacting iron ore dispatches and staff vehicle movement, thereby causing substantial financial losses to the company.
Additionally, the two water tankers—bearing registration numbers KA 43 4660 and KA 13 B 0950—were illegally parked on the property with the apparent intent to obstruct iron ore transportation. After further discussions, the road was finally cleared at approximately 17:15 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Property Related at BALCO site at 24 Jun 2025, 6:31 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 24.06.2025, at 1831 hrs during CCTV Monitoring, CSOC Operator observed suspicious material loading in vehicle CG 12 BN 6183 & hence, notified all Security Posts to halt the vehicle. At around 1840 hrs, at the Alumina 4W Gate, security personnel found unauthorized material being carried inside vehicle CG12 BN 6183, driven by Mr. Lakshmi Das, from M/s ABB India Ltd. The material was issued by Store In-Charge Mr. Dilip Nak (UID-1609312), also from M/s ABB India Ltd. Both individuals were involved in this incident. An immediate investigation was initiated. The unauthorized material, including a termination kit and 2Ug kit, valued at approximately Rs.12,524/- was seized and retained at Plant-1. Written statements were taken from Mr. Lakshmi Das and Mr. Dilip Nak. The matter has been escalated to the concerned HR department for further action.

INCIDENTDETAILS: A local leader from Basantapada was on strike at Main gate as some of the villagers did not get their compensation after losing their lands.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at RDC site at 24 Oct 2025, 10:57 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 24.10.2025 at DSC, around 10:57 hrs, while entering the Parking Plaza, the driver (Ramraj Gurjar) of an empty Zinc Cathode vehicle NL01AJ0607)M/s Maheshwari Logistics hit the Parking Plaza boom barrier, resulting boom barrier got damaged. The driver’s alcohol test was found negative. Driver's statement has been recorded and blocked in the SAP,  an email has been sent to the concerned department.

INCIDENTTITLE: Chitradurga shift vehicles are stopped in Hireguntanuru at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 10 Dec 2025, 5:25 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 10.12.2025, Chitradurga Shift buses were stopped at Hireguntanuru beginning at approximately 0525 hrs. The blockage was initiated by Girish and several local panchayat members (including Manjanna), who demanded job opportunities, funds for temple work, and access to drinking water facilities. The situation was reported to the ER/PR department, and an official police complaint was lodged (112). Police officers responded to the location and engaged in discussions with the villagers. The intervention led to a temporary resolution of the issue, and the shift vehicles were released by 0745 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: SOP- LSR Violation at CLZS(CHANDERIYA) site at 18 Oct 2025, 2:40 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 14:40 hrs. a physical altercation occurred today between two L&T Business Partners near the Hydro-2 Circle (Walkway area).
During the incident, an argument broke out between Md. Afsar Shah (GP: CH60016728) and Shoaib (GP: CH60016744), which escalated into a fight. In the heat of the moment, Shoaib used a metal bangle (kada) to hit Md. Afsar Shah on the head, resulting in bleeding.
Injured person was immediately taken to the dispensary for medical attention. Md. Afsar Shah sustained a head injury and, after first aid, was referred to Sanwariya Government Hospital for further treatment.
As part of the investigation:
Drug tests for both were conducted and found negative.
Alcohol tests showed positive results:
Md. Afsar Shah – 0.172 mg/L
Shoaib – 0.106 mg/L

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at RAM AGUCHA site at 2025-10-26 18:35
INCIDENTDETAILS: Dated 26.10.2025, at around 1500 Hrs, after unloading the vehicle in plant, driver Shariful sheikh parked the Orica magazine vehicle JH 01 DT 1173 near the Orica plant and went outside for snacks. At 1835 hrs, when he came back to move his vehicle to a designated parking spot, he discovered that both the batteries from his vehicle were missing. This was informed to security and CCTV recordings of nearby installed camera were checked but no movement was observed. The vehicle belonged to the business partners. Similar incident was reported in the same area on the night of 24.10.2025. On the morning of 25.10.2025, when the driver Jabir Hussain of vehicle JH09AH0696 came to move the vehicle, he discovered that the batteries of the vehicle were stolen.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at IRON AND POWER site at 2025-07-01 16:55
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1655 hrs, Rajbir Singh, an employee at the Oxygen Plant, reported to the security team that a person named Moti Lal Mahto (Gate Pass No. 78590), affiliated with Nova Air and a resident of Siyaljori village, had entered the oxygen plant premises under the influence of alcohol.
Upon receiving information regarding the intoxicated individual at the oxygen plant, the QRT team promptly arrived at the scene and conducted an alcohol test. The result showed a BAC 1.40%. The individual punch-blocked for the past four months due to medical leave. After obtaining a written statement from him, the security team escorted him out of the plant at 17:50 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Property Related at BALCO site at 22 Jul 2025, 3:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 22nd July 2025 at 1530 hrs., during frisking security guard Mr. Abhishek Giri observed and reported Suspicious activity involving Mr. Krish Baghel (80000014299) an employee of M/s Amit Engineering at Project Gate. The individual was suspected of attempting to conceal company tool one Chain Puli in his bag. Further inquiry revealed that the one Chain Puli had been sourced from the company store. The matter was investigated and crossed checked with side supervisors also where it was found that Supervisor Mr. Md Arif has given his Chain Puli to work at side and order him to keep in store as well after completing the work. But Mr. Baghel didn't keep the material in store and reached the project gate with intention of pilferages. During questioning by Shift In charge , Mr. Krish Baghel admitted to the act of pilferage. The recovered material from Mr. Krish Baghel , has been seized. Statements of Mr. Krish Baghel and his Supervisor Mr. Md Arif has been recorded for reference. Gate Passes of Mr. Krish Baghel has been confiscated. The matter has been escalated to the concerned HR for further action.

INCIDENTTITLE: Shift vehicles are stopped in Haliyuru at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2025-08-03 13:20
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 03.08.2025 at 13:20 PM, a group led by Naganna obstructed shift vehicles at Haliyur village demanding job opportunities. After notification, the matter was referred to ER/PR for discussion. A police team was available at the spot to maintain order and facilitate the talks. After discussion in the presence of police personnel and security team, the issue was temporarily resolved and the shift buses were released at 1:45 PM.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 12 Jul 2025, 11:55 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Around 2355 hrs when suspicious activity observed near Baba gate, then searching in the area was carried out. Some scrap material in the jungle near perimeter- 321 was found, which is recovered and dumped behind the security office complex.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 13 Jul 2025, 12:15 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Around 0015 hrs shift officer received a call from the CCR shift incharge that there were around five to six miscreants at the backside of the silo area and they were roaming around in the Silo area. At that time QRT team with shift officer is patrolling central store back side area , they immediately reached there, searched all the silo backside and front side areas but could not find anything, but during the searching AHP security civil guard Gursewak Singh informed that five to six miscreants had taken some scrap material from Simar office side and fled inside the forest area. The QRT team immediately reached the backside of Simar office and followed the miscreants after seeing their footprints, after much searching through the forest area we recovered the scrap material, but the miscreants had fled from there, we found their one bag along with the scrap material and also found three helmets which the miscreants had used to enter the plant. All the items were kept along with the scrap material at the backside of the security building.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at RDC site at 10 Nov 2025, 9:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: at SKM,, The Mining Control Room observed 02 thieves were seen the PCH-2 Scrapyard area which was reported immediately to Gate I/C ASO Naryan Singh. Upon receiving this information. INCIDENTTITLE: Select at RDC site at 10 Nov 2025, 11:25 PM

Gate I/C ASO immediately dispatched the security team to the spot. On seeing the security team, the 02 unidentified persons hid in the bushes(outside the B/W) and fled. The security team conducted a thorough search of the entire area.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at TUTICORIN site at 03 Dec 2025, 10:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Incident Date & Time: 03.12.2025 at 10:00 hrs
Location: Refinery Area – Cell House 
Description of the Incident
On 2nd Dec at 17:00 hrs a phone call was received from Mr. Prince Prakasam, Lead - Mechanical to Mr. Selvakumar, JSO stating that some bare footprints found near slag yard, Mr. Selvakumar reached the spot, took the photos of footprints and informed to Hod.
On 3rd December 2025 at 10:00 hrs, a phone call was received from Mr. Prince Prakasam again stating that four copper raisers were found missing from the Refinery cell house.
•	Approx. length per raiser: 5 meters
•	Weight per raiser: 120 kg
•	Estimated value per copper raiser: ?1,00,000
A team comprising Mr. Prince Prakasam, Mrs. Judy Fernando, Mr. Sankaranarayanan, Mr. Saravanakumar, and Mr. Selvakumar visited the spot for observation. The team found bare footprints leading towards the PAP–SLF boundary wall and drag marks.
On same day search started in and around the refinery area, at 16:30hrs the team comprising Mr. Saravanakumar, Mr. Vijayaraj & Mr. Albert Jebastin, located four copper raisers in the refinery cell house platforms.
Again, on next day (4th Dec) a phone call was received from Mr. Prince Prakasam stating that three copper raisers were missing, Head Security contacted Mr. Saravanan GM on phone and asked him to confirm how many numbers were missing. He asked for time for couple of days to check the entire site for his team to confirm. 
On 8th Dec Head Security again contacted GM Saravanan at 9:36 am to confirm the incident and he confirmed that three raisers were missing.
On 8th and 9th of Dec’25 searching conducted by security team across the plant in and around the boundary wall and nothing could be located and was informed to COO.
On 9th of Dec ’25 COO, along with Mr. Ramesh Ganesh, Head-Inst., visited the site along with Head security, duty SO Mr. Saravanakumar and user team Mr. Prince Prakasam and Mr. Sankaranarayanan, inspection started at 16:30hrs entire refinery, SLF road, PAP slag yard and up to boundary wall.
Senior team concluded that it could be theft and decided to file a legal complaint.
Additional Observations:
•	No CCTV coverage in the area from Refinery to SLF.
•	No manning in that area since 14.07.2024 due to manpower optimization.
•	Concertina coil was missing (50 mtrs.) near SLF boundary wall (height: 8 feet). 
Findings
1.	Bare footprints from Refinery to SLF boundary wall.
2.	Evidence of drag markings.
3.	Missing concertina coil near SLF boundary wall (height: 8 feet).
Immediate Action Taken
1.	Immediate deployment of 2 guards at Refinery area during dark hours (6 PM to 6 AM).
2.	Concertina coil fixed in the vulnerable area.
3.	Continuous random security patrolling by Duty SO ensured.
4.	Awareness created among guards regarding rounds and whistle blowing every 30 minutes at duty posts to act as deterrence.
Recommendations
1.	Increase lighting in critical areas.
2.	Install CCTV cameras at locations where valuable materials are stored.
3.	Ensure Duty SO has control and monitoring of manpower movement.
4.	Restrict multiple entry access points.
5.	Prepare a checklist of valuable materials and conduct frequent physical verification by user department.
6.	Deploy one security guard round-the-clock in high-value material areas.
7.	Police assistance is being sought through our PR team.
8.	Detailed report will be submitted later as further investigation is in progress.
Spotters:
•	Mr. Prince Prakasam
•	Mr. Sankaranarayanan
Security Team (Incident Response):
•	Mrs. J. Judy Fernando
•	Mr. SaravanaKumar
•	Mr. G Selvakumar
Attachment: - Photocopy of the pictures.

report an attempted theft incident that occurred in the early hours of 26th August 2025 near the BICO Washing Plant.At approximately 3:45 AM, the owner of a small shop located near the mines approached Mr. Bijoy Kumar Giri, the security personnel stationed at the BICO IN gate. INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 03 Nov 2025, 10:20 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:

The attempt was thwarted due to the timely presence and intervention of the Security Shift In-Charge and QRT-4 team.
During the attempted intrusion at the Garga DG boundary, the thieves began pelting stones, which was retaliated by the Security team, forcing them to flee into the nearby wooded area. No loss has been reported. Subsequently, the foxhole created in the boundary was temporarily sealed using a CGI sheet.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 02 Nov 2025, 3:05 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 02.11.2025 at around 03:05 hrs., an intelligence input received indicating suspicious activity between Alumina Gate and Project Gate, prompting immediate communication to the NDO Officer, Plant-1 In charge, and the Quick Response Team (QRT). The security team conducted a search operation and recovered 15 pieces of aluminium flexible sheets and bus bars weighing approximately 160 kg, valued at ?32,000/-, scattered in nearby bushes. Although the suspects escaped, the coordinated efforts of the security personnel prevented further theft, ensured the situation was contained without escalation, and the recovered items have been securely stored in the Plant-1 Security Store.

INCIDENTTITLE: The workshop entrance was blocked by an individual named Manju from Meghalahalli 05 Dec 2025, 3:50 PM

Quick Response Team (QRT) personnel were diverted to the location, and the ER/PR dept. was notified to take corrective action. Following a discussion, the issue was temporarily resolved, and the roadblock was cleared at 1625 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 11 Nov 2025, 8:05 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 11.11.2025 at 2005 hours, an intelligence input indicated the suspected involvement of Mr. Parmeshwar Urawn (Unique ID: 8000018907), an employee of M/s Larsen & Toubro Limited, in the unauthorized removal of two copper patties concealed inside his jacket. Acting on the alert, CSOC immediately informed the Plant 1/3 In-Charge and placed security personnel at Project Gate, Parsabhata Gate, and Alumina Gate on high alert. During shift movement at Project Gate, the security team intercepted Mr. Urawn while allegedly attempting to exit the premises. A thorough frisking led to the recovery of two copper patties worth approximately ?4,000 from his jacket, and his gate pass was confiscated. The recovered items and gate pass were seized, statements of other involved individuals were recorded, and their gate passes were also withheld for verification. The matter has been escalated to the HR Department for action as per company policy, and the recovered items have been securely stored in the Plant-1 Security Store.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 11 Nov 2025, 8:20 AM

Acting on the alert, CSOC immediately informed the Plant 1 and Plant 3 In-Charge and placed security personnel at Parsabhata Gate and Alumina Gate on high alert. During shift movement at Alumina Gate, the security team intercepted Mr. Khande while allegedly attempting to exit with the concealed items. A thorough frisking led to the recovery of 18 Aluminium plates worth approximately ?2,000 from his bag, and his gate pass was confiscated. INCIDENTTITLE: Transport stoppage at SMS gate
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 21-04-2025 at approximately 10:05hrs, local residents from Pilgao and Sarmanas gathered at the Sarmanas gate and blocked the transportation of iron ore. The agitation was triggered due to unresolved local issues, including farmer-related concerns and property disputes specific to the Sarmanas area. The local communities had earlier communicated a resolution deadline of 20th April 2025 to the company. As the issues remained unaddressed by the stipulated date, the villagers resorted to a transport blockade as a form of protest. The situation was brought under control by the local Police Sub-Inspector (PSI). In response, company officials including the CSR Head, Deputy CEO, and Cluster Head arrived at the site and held discussions with the protestors. The Mamlatdar was also contacted and later joined the deliberations.

Following prolonged discussions, an understanding was reached, and transportation activities were resumed at around 1215 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2025-06-03 17:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On dated 03.06.2025, at around 1730 hrs, G shift vehicles were stopped at Bommennavagathihalli village by a group of villagers led by Sasi, Keshav, and Ashok for their various demand like job, dust compensation. The incident was reported to ER/PR, CSR and Sirigere police station for support. 
Multiple rounds of discussion happened at location by Vedanta employees and Bus owners however village people were not agree to release the vehicles.
At around 18:12Hrs two Police persons reached at location and tried to convinced the village peoples. 
Police complaint lodged at emergency number 112 and their team also mobilized. At around 1930Hrs, one more Police staff from Sirigere Police Station reached at location and try to convinced village peoples. 
Admin team arranged alternative vehicles, and all employees moved from the location at around 20:30Hrs. 
 Later FIR was lodged at Bharamsagara police station for the incident.

Corrective Action
1.	Matter informed to all concerned persons.
2.	Matter inform to Sirigere, Bharamsagar Police Station and 112.
3.	Matter discussed with village peoples by Vedanta Employees, Bus Owners and Police Team
4.	FIR was Lodged in Bharamsagar Police Station 

Preventive Action
1.	Better coordination with village peoples and stake holder.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 04 Dec 2025, 10:15 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 22:15 hrs, CCTV operators observed some individuals loitering outside the boundary wall near Garga Yard-2, appearing to have intentions of committing theft. The CRO immediately alerted QRT-4 and the Security Shift In-Charge. Both the Security Shift IC and the QRT-4 team promptly reached the location. Upon arrival, they faced stone pelting from the miscreants and took necessary countermeasures. Eventually, the miscreants concealed themselves in nearby bushes and fled the area at around 01:15 hrs. No material loss or injuries were reported.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Political/ Social at VAL J site at 28 May 2025, 12:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Around 1230hrs, a villager was traveling with his family on a bike near Shiv Rampur Chowk. At that time, an ash-loaded vehicle overtook him. A person from the village, taking offense, stopped the movement of vehicles on the public road. The QRT reached the spot, explained the situation to the villagers, and counseled the driver of the ash vehicle. Our security guard was instructed to take strict action if such incidents occur in the future. The movement on the road was resumed at 1243hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 09 Dec 2025, 10:40 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 2240 hours, three individuals entered the premises near Tower 17 of the Linde Company Office by cutting the concertina wire and attempted to steal approximately 15 meters of aluminum wire. A security guard deployed in the Linde Company Office area observed the activity and immediately alerted QRT-2. Upon receiving the information, QRT-2 promptly reached the location. INCIDENTTITLE: John Mines Canter drivers caused two separate roadblocks at Gate #3, 6:50 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: John Mines Canter drivers caused two separate roadblocks at Gate #3, demanding continuous and uncontrolled access into the mine site. The first blockade started at 06:50 hrs and was temporarily cleared by 07:35 hrs after informing the ER/PR and Operations teams. The second, more serious blockade began at 08:00 hrs. Drivers also gathered at the Admin Block by 08:45 hrs. Due to blockage and traffic jam, shift vehicles were stopped inside mines near gate#3.The situation was finally resolved at 09:25 hrs after a discussion involving Mr. Somasekar Reddy, Mr. Ravi Nayaka ,Mr. Kiran & the security team, which successfully convinced the drivers to clear the road. The persistent demand for uncontrolled entry caused a major access disruption from 06:50 hrs until 09:25 hrs

INCIDENTTITLE: Unauthorized entry/ Intrusion/Trespassing at RAM AGUCHA site at 11 Jan 2026, 9:37 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 2137 hrs, guard SG Swai Singh went to inspect the boring machine at Deco-1 B area. While conducting the inspection, he noticed three unidentified intruders inside the premises collecting scrap materials. The intruders were seen picking up cables, iron pieces, and pipes, and placing them into bags, presumably for theft. On realizing they had been observed, the intruders immediately abandoned the items and fled the area towards the nearby jungle. The Solar QRT was immediately deployed and thoroughly checked the entire area, but the intruders could not be located.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR - Worker/ Union/ Transporters at ENABLING site at 11 Dec 2025, 2:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 14:50 hrs, QRT-1 reported that approximately 30 to 40 Raiyats (working under Maa Vindhyavashani) were moving towards Baghabandh Railway Crossing with the intention of blocking the railway track, protesting their reassignment to different contractors from BPO. The CRO immediately informed the Security Shift In-Charge, the security team, and Siyaljori Police Station. Upon receiving the information, the security team promptly reached the spot and successfully prevented the attempt to block the railway track. Subsequently, at 16:35 hrs, the group was escorted to the HR Conference Hall for a meeting with Dy. CHRO, Mr. Sanjay Kumar Sinha (PR), and Mr. Dharmendra Kumar (IR), in the presence of the police and security teams. The management advised them and scheduled a follow-up meeting on 12.12.2025 at 11:00 hrs for the final discussion.

INCIDENTTITLE: Offsite Incidents at RAM AGUCHA site at 2025-05-08 22:22
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 08/05/2025, the B shift employee bus (RJ 09 PB 8557) departed from the plant at 2218 hrs. At approximately 2222 hrs, a call was received at the control room from one of the employees on board, reporting a stone-pelting incident near the Mool Ji Ka Kheda Toll Plaza. According to the report, 2–3 unidentified individuals, standing near the toll plaza, pelted stones at the approaching bus and fled the scene on a motorcycle immediately after the act.

The Quick Response Team (QRT) was immediately dispatched to the location. The bus continued to the colony and was accompanied by the QRT shortly after. One employee sustained a minor injury to the hand during the incident.

Local police were promptly informed. On 09/05/2025, the bus driver and supervisor formally lodged a complaint regarding the incident with the police.

INCIDENTTITLE: Road #1 truck movement of MEL stopped by Maruthi from V.Palya village at 21 Apr 2025, 8:45 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 21.04.2025 at around 0845 hrs, Road #1 truck movement of MEL stopped by Maruthi from V.Palya village due to land dispute and Chitradurga BP bus contract termination without any intimation.
Upon received information, alerted security team and informed to admin to divert shift vehicles via king gate and deployed additional security staff and bouncers at the location.
At around 0955 hrs, Informed to Chitradurga Rural Police CI and Hiregruntanur police station by ER/PR.
At around 1000 hrs, JCB brought by Maruthi and same was stopped at BBH Rly siding security gate.
At around 1045 hrs, Maruti also stopped the Road 4 operation and after discussion by security team, cleared from siding area and movement resumed at 1055 hrs, however BSV ground staff not ready to continue the operations and same was communicated to logistics department.
At around 1345 hrs, Police team reached at BBH Rly siding and discussed the matter with Vedanta (Mr. Ravi Nayaka & Mr. Ambarish) and Maruthi.
At around 1545 hrs, MEL Manjunath available at location to briefed about the land details to police.
Post discussion, police advised to lodge police complaint and bring the railway Siding land documents to the police station dated 22.04.2025 for the discussion by all parties.
Also Mr. Arun Shamanur told not to start the siding operation till matter is not resolved.

On 22.04.2025 at around 0935 hrs, again Maruthi came to road #1 BBH siding and stopped the MEL Movement.
At around 1040 hrs, Road #4 truck movement of Vedanta stopped by Maruthi.
Upon received information, alerted security team and deployed additional security staff and bouncers at the location.
At around 1230 hrs, diverted & approx. 50 personnel are available at location (Vedanta Onroll, Workmen, BP Staff including Security & bouncers)
At around 1300 hrs, joint discussion done with Maruthi by Vedanta, MEL team   at BBH Rly Siding office. Meanwhile Chitradurga Rural Police Station CI with his staff and reached at BBH Rly Siding office at 1320 hr.
Joint discussion in presence of police staff done at 1325 hrs.
CI discussed this matter and told to Maruthi for the following points.
1.Do not block the road till issue resolved.
2.Compalint to be given SP Office for the issue by Maruthi and joint survey to be carried out with Govt’ Survey.
Post discussion, Road #4 movement resumed at around 1340 hrs.
At around 1710 hrs, Security, Rly Logistics and ER/PR team visited Chitradurga Rural Police station and met CI.
CI suggested to file one application for the Govt. Survey to be conducted on priority for the land and survey application copy to be handover to CI also.

On 25 November 2025 at approximately 12:45 Hrs, Truck No.GA04T 9333 of Joshi Transport - was blocked by the Coal Logistics department based on involvement in gross weight manipulation (Approx 20 Kg) for internal shifting vehicle from Jetty to MCD coal yard. Same was confirmed with CCTV footage that driver was involved intentionally by getting down from vehicle during gross weighment at weighbridge to avoid unloading of excess 20kg material. 
Due to above involvement of driver, Same vehicle was blocked in system and not allowed for further shifting of material. However, driver refused to accept the decision and obstructed vehicle movement at Round 15:10Hrs along with other drivers at 3 & 4 Amona Jetty loading Area.

Despite detailed discussions with Head Coal Logistics Mr. Adam Khan, CSO VAB Mr. Rohit Kumar, and SO Ms. Priyanka Singh, no resolution was reached, prompting CSR Head Mr. Prasad Ajgaonkar and SO Mr. Govind Singh Bhati to intervene, though the matter remained unresolved. Following confirmation from the user department, the control room informed the police, who visited the site at around 17:46 Hrs.
Subsequently, CEO MCD Mr. Umesh Khade, along with the above-mentioned employees, held further discussions with the drivers, after which the blocked vehicle was unblocked and normal movement resumed at around 18:50 hrs.

Action Taken – 
1.	Reached at incident site and discuss the matter with drivers
2.	Informed shared with all related persons.
3.	Based on confirmation from CEO MCD , Police team called at location.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 24 Nov 2025, 3:05 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 25th Nov,25, at around 0305hrs, while routine patrolling by township QRT team, security officers spotted one individual trying to hide near bungalow S1, at the same time 2 more individuals were spotted. Security officer immediately responded to catch them, when theft encountered security team, they were trying to escape from the site. Security chased the individuals, at the same time while escaping, thefts were throwing stones at security team, immediately security officer informed QRT and Security Control Room for back up. All plant QRT and Baaz reached the spot, they searched the area, but thieves were not spotted. Security team then searched the S1 Bungalow to identify if any theft has occurred. After searching the bungalow, stones were found at dinning area of the bungalow. Nothing has been stolen from the site. Furthermore, other bungalows S2, S3 and S4 have been searched, and no intrusion were identified. The area was searched and entry point of the theft. On same date, at around 0805 hrs, duty officer informed SCR that theft had occurred in bungalow S5. Security team reached the location to secure the crime area and identify the stolen material. Informed Badmal police for further investigation.

RCA- Perimeter breach due to damaged concertina coil and insufficient preventive surveillance, enabling unauthorized entry near S1.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 25 Nov 2025, 11:21 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1121 hrs, a call was received from the Water Reservoir guard regarding suspicious movement of 4–5 miscreants near the location. Immediately, the QRT team and BAZZ team responded to the spot. However, the miscreants fled from the location. A thorough search of the entire area was conducted, during which 22 pieces of scrap material, weighing approximately 600 kg, were found. Guards have been instructed to remain more alert and vigilant in the area.

RCA- Lack of continuous surveillance and inadequate deterrence measures allowed miscreants to approach the reservoir area unnoticed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at ENABLING site at 08 Nov 2025, 5:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 1750 hrs, near Sierra Post, an unidentified individual, suspected to be under the influence of alcohol, approached from the Rajwar Tola side and engaged in aggressive behavior by pelting stones at the on-duty security guard, Ram Swaroop Yadav.
The QRT team and Security Shift In-Charge responded promptly, reached the location, and escorted the individual back to Rajwar Tola village. At approximately 1830 hrs, a group of 4 to 6 women and children, reportedly family members of the individual, arrived at Sierra Post. The situation was handled calmly, and the group was persuaded by QRT-1, QRT-2, and the Security Shift In-Charge to return to Rajwar Tola village. Later, at around 1920 hrs, a Police team visited Sierra Post, conducted inquiries with the on-duty security guard and QRT-1, and subsequently departed.

INCIDENTTITLE: The shift vehicle are stopped by Jayaram in Palya  gate at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 03 Dec 2025, 5:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 03.12.2025 at around 0530 hrs, shift vehicles stationed in Chitradurga were prevented from proceeding at the Palya Gate by an individual identified as Jayaram, who appeared to be intoxicated. Jayaram was making unsubstantiated claims for compensation concerning land on BBH Siding Road #4.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at VAL J site at 24 Nov 2025, 9:54 AM

INCIDENTTITLE: Fraud/Malpractice at ENABLING site at 09 Nov 2025, 8:10 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0810 hrs, the Security Shift IC received information that vehicle number WB31N 0281, belonging to Guru Prasad Carrier, Illawara, Haldia Port, along with its driver, was taken into security custody for suspicion of material tampering.
"Upon receiving the information, the security team intercepted and detained the vehicle at the RMHS Weigh Bridge. During the preliminary investigation, the driver stated that he had not tampered with the material in any way. The driver was held in security custody at the RMHS Gate for further investigation.
Subsequently, the driver was re-interrogated in coordination with the Logistics Team, during which he confessed to tampering with the materials at Bakura, under instructions from the vehicle owner. A written statement was obtained from him by the security team, and the relevant authorities have been informed.
According to the transporter, an FIR will be filed tomorrow at around 1000 hrs. Until then, the vehicle must remain detained at the gate. This information was confirmed by the Police Station Munshi, as discussed with Mr. Sanjay Sinha.  "

INCIDENTTITLE: IR - Worker/ Union/ Transporters at ENABLING site at 25 Nov 2025, 10:15 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 10:15 hrs, CCTV operators reported that around 30–40 Raiyats(OJT) had gathered in front of the IR Office. The gathering appears to be in response to their recent reassignment under different contractors. The Raiyats are currently unwilling to join the new contractors, stating that they wish to continue working exclusively in the BPO.
CRO promptly informed the Security Shift IC and QRT team, who immediately reached the location. Initially, there was no discussion between IR Head Mr. Dharmendra Kumar and the Raiyats. Around 12:42 hrs, the Raiyats blocked the railway track at Tango-18 railway crossing (near central store) and remained there until a meeting was held at 15:00 hrs between Maa Vindhyavasni Raiyat workers and management at the Maithon office, under the supervision of Siyaljori Police Station In-Charge and workers (leaded by Shekhar Choubey (JMM-Dhanadabar),unis sai and others . Following the meeting, the track was cleared at 17:30 hrs. Committee have taken 15 days to address the issue, with the next meeting scheduled for 11.12.2025 and it is decided that  written assurance will be provided by Mr. Dharmendra Kumar on 26th Nov 2025 in the presence of the Police Station In-Charge. The security team was present throughout the incident, closely monitoring the situation and prepared to respond to any untoward activity.  All workers left at around 18:00 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at SILVASSA site at 2025-04-16 17:40
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 16th April 2025. At approx. 1740hrs, CSG Gulshed Khan was conducting routine bag checks for workers exiting the premises. During this process, one worker, Mr. Sandeep Yadav, a Technician of AC repair and working under M/S Network Office Automation (a sub-contractor of M/S Blue Star, which is a sub-contractor of M/S Monomark Engineering), appeared visibly uncomfortable during the frisking. Upon conducting a more thorough inspection, 10 pieces of copper shorting switch contacts and one insulated copper cable were discovered concealed in his tool bag. CSG Gulshed Khan immediately informed SCRO and information was swiftly passed to Dy CSO Heramb Joshi and CSO G Mallesham. Dy CSO along with Chief Security Officer conducted an enquiry and it came to know that Sandeep Yadav was working for AC repairing job in Tank House area where unattended copper shorting switches and insulated copper cables were also kept by E&I team for tank house related work. Acting further Sandeep Yadav was blacklisted and a police complaint was submitted by security BP.

Despite their escape, the coordinated security response prevented loss and ensured the situation was contained without escalation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Dharna at CLZS(CHANDERIYA) site at 18 Dec 2025, 10:15 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 18.12.2025, at around 10:15 Hrs,  a local resident, Parmeshwar Jat of Ajoliya Ka Kheda, who alleged that the death of his buffalo was due to Bhunetiya caused by lead pollution from plant operations. Despite initial counselling and engagement, the individual arrived at the main gate with the deceased animal on a tractor-trolley, creating a sensitive law-and-order situation.
Timely intimation to the police ensured their presence at the site. As a precaution, the outer gate was temporarily closed to prevent any forceful entry, while one side of the gate remained operational, ensuring no disruption to routine movement or plant operations. Discussion was held in the presence of CI Gangrar, during which following demands were raised-
1.	Provision of a round-the-clock veterinary doctor at Ajoliya Ka Kheda.
2.	Insurance coverage for domestic animals, sponsored by HZL.
3.	Consideration of the construction of Veterinary Hospital in the vicinity.
They sought the response from HZL within 7 days as the situation was very tense and Parmeshwar Jat produced a bottle containing petrol and threatened self-immolation while standing on the tractor seat. The police acted immediately, seized the petrol bottle, counselled the individual, and successfully defused the situation, preventing any untoward incident.
No written commitment was given, but to de-escalate the situation, it was informed that the matter to be discussed with senior Management and At around 13:15 hrs, the agitators dispersed peacefullyThis is submitted for your kind information and necessary directions to the concerned.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR -  Transporters block the gate 01 tipper movement at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 24 Dec 2025, 5:45 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"Incident Summary: Gate#1 Tipper Movement Blockade. 
On 24.12.2025, Gate #1 movement of vedanta and John mines were disrupted by a protest involving new tipper owners and drivers seeking loading permissions. The situation required intervention from logistics, local transport associations, and law enforcement before resuming in the afternoon.

Timeline of Events
 * 06:45 hrs: New tipper owners and drivers halted all Vedanta and John Mines vehicle movements at Gate #1. The logistics department was immediately notified to take corrective action.
 * 07:50 hrs: Protesters erected a temporary shelter (shamiyana) at Muthugudur village. Local authorities at the Chikkajajur police station were alerted.
 * Gate #1 Association members engaged in discussions with the protesters to mediate the blockade and involve the Matha in resolving the dispute.
 * 13:30 hrs: Police personnel arrived on-site to oversee negotiations.
 * 15:30 hrs: Following successful deliberations, the blockade was lifted and normal tipper movement resumed.
Key Meeting Resolutions
To temporarily resolve the conflict, the following points were agreed upon:
 * Immediate Release: All currently loaded and halted tippers are cleared for transit for the day.
 * Loading Restrictions: Effective tomorrow, loading permissions will be restricted to local tippers only; non-local vehicles will be barred.
 * Resolution Window: Association members have been granted a three-week timeline to find a permanent solution regarding loading permissions for the new tipper owners."

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 04 Nov 2025, 9:16 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 04.11.2025 at around 21:16 hrs., during routine CCTV surveillance, the Central Security Operations Centre (CSOC) team detected three unidentified individuals breaching the boundary wall near Watch Tower-8 from the plant side. The incident was promptly reported to the nearest security guard, Plant-2 Shift In charge, and the Quick Response Team (QRT). The security team immediately launched a search operation inside and outside the boundary wall, but the intruders fled upon sensing security presence, abandoning some material outside. The team recovered 12 PCS sheets from the site, while the miscreants escaped with 12 PCS (50 kg) of sheets valued at ?1,250/-. Despite their escape, the swift and coordinated response ensured no further property loss and the situation was contained without escalation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Unauthorized entry/ Intrusion/Trespassing at CLZS(CHANDERIYA) site at 15 May 2025, 1:20 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Detection and Initial Observation
? At approximately 01:20 hrs., Mr. Manoranjan, the CCTV operator on duty,
observed suspicious movement involving four individuals near the outer side
of the coal yard boundary wall near Tango 13. The individuals appeared to
be inspecting the perimeter and preparing to enter the premises.
? Immediately upon observing the activity, CCTV operator informed the QRTBAAZ,
ASO, and other Security Team. Following this, the security team
swiftly mobilized to respond to the threat.
Response and Ambush
? Following the alert, the security team assembled swiftly and, under the
direction of the Security Officer, planned a strategic ambush.
? Security personnel positioned themselves around the target area to cover all
possible escape routes and awaited further action from the intruders.
Unauthorized Entry and Theft Attempt (01:37 hrs.)
? At approximately 01:37 hrs., two intruders scaled the boundary wall and
entered the premises.
? They proceeded to a waste dump area containing scrap material of value
(lead dross).
? The intruders began filling bag with the lead dross and threw the bag over
the wall to their two accomplices waiting outside.
? This theft attempt was closely monitored by the ambush team.
Apprehension of Intruders (02:05 hrs)
? While the theft was ongoing, the ambush team and QRT moved in.
? Upon seeing the security personnel, the intruders attempted to flee.
? The security team gave immediate chase and apprehended both intruders
within the premises.
? Meanwhile, the accomplices outside began pelting stones at the security
personnel in an effort to aid the escape.
? The security team responded accordingly to deter the attackers, after which
the accomplices outside fled the scene under cover of darkness.
? No major injuries were reported, and the situation was brought under
control.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 2025-05-10 02:30
INCIDENTDETAILS: On May 10, 2025, at approximately 0230 hours, the CSOC, during CCTV surveillance, detected the presence of 05 unauthorized individuals near the APP IN area. INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 24 May 2025, 2:18 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 24.05.2025, at approximately 1418 hrs., during Security frisking at Parsabhata Gate, security personnel noticed suspicious behavior from Mr. Vijay Kumar (UID: 80000005617), a worker from M/s AIF Engineering Pvt. Ltd., a subcontractor under SLD Brothers Enterprises. Upon inspection, 12-meter copper wire, value of Rs. 400/- was found concealed in his bag. Mr. Vijay Kumar claimed the material was for personal use. The incident was immediately reported to the Plant 3 Zonal In-Charge, and a detailed investigation confirmed that Mr. Vijay Kumar was attempting to sell the copper wire out of the plant. During questioning, Mr. Vijay Kumar admitted to the act of pilferage. His gate pass was confiscated, and the matter was escalated to the concerned HR department for further action. Statements were recorded from both Mr. Vijay Kumar and his supervisor, Mr. Suraj Manha, for documentation.
