INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 23 May 2025, 5:56 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 23.05.2025 , at approximately 1756 hrs, an intelligence input was received from a security guard regarding suspicious behaviour by a worker carrying a copper bundle in his bag. Upon noticing Security Frisking activity at the Parsabhata Exit Gate, the worker discreetly dropped the material near Parsabhata ROB Road and exited the premises. The Quick Response Team (QRT) and Zone In-Charge, Mr. Abesh Bundela, were immediately informed and they promptly reached the location to investigate where they recovered the mentioned material. Verification through CCTV footage and punch-in/punch-out records confirmed the identity of the individual as Mr. Shaniram Agariya (UID: 1645568), a carpenter employed under M/s Larsen & Toubro Limited. The recovered material, a copper bundle weighing approximately 0.5 kg and valued at ?200, was seized. The incident has been escalated to the concerned HR department for further disciplinary action and necessary proceedings.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at ENABLING site at 11 Jan 2026, 5:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1700 hrs on 11/01/2026, an empty truck (WB 23F 5321, Transporter: Amrit Freight Carriers), after unloading BF coke, met with an accident about 200 meters ahead of Alkusha more on the government main road while proceeding towards Bokaro.
 The truck collided with pedestrian, Sukhdev Rajwar, a bike mechanic and resident of Bhuiyan Dwarika, Alkusha, died on the spot. The vehicle was intercepted and detained by local villagers, and a partially block the road near Alkusha More.
On 12/01/2026 morning, villagers did not allow any vehicles to pass and are demanding compensation from the company. The incident was formally reported to the Chas Mufassil and Siyaljori Police Stations along with SDPO & SDO by Public relations department. On 12/01/2026, the company task force comprising PR, IR, CSR, and Security teams, along with police team, engaged with the villagers. 
The roadblock was cleared at 16:00 hrs. after a mutual agreement was reached in the presence of police at Chas mufassil police station .
As part of the settlement, on behalf of M/s Amrit Freight Carrier ( Transporter) agreed to provide ?5 lakh as compensation and offer Contractual employment to the deceased’s wife on humanitarian ground since the Village is in nearby community.

INCIDENTDETAILS: During the alcohol check at Gate 02, Chitradurga shift bus driver Siddesh (DL No: KA16 200005249) was found alcohol positive (3%) while operating vehicle KA16 AB 1139. The security team stopped the driver and denied entry into the mines.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft attempt foiled at RMF.
INCIDENTDETAILS: During patrolling the RMF area one gas cutter with cylinder found. (intel source)

INCIDENTTITLE: Obstructed the road near sannappa land at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 15 Jul 2025, 8:55 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 15.07.2025 at around 0855 hrs, kumar  of meghalahalli  Village obstructed the road near sannappa land by demand of water tanker contract.Post discussion by ER/PR & Security, issue temperorily resolved and shift vehicles released at  0905 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Traffic at ENABLING site at 2025-06-13 20:30
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 2030 hrs, The Karma Gate Commander received information that vehicle No. JH01CP-5437 (a bus belonging to M C Kutty Contractor, used to transport plant workers from Hundru) lightly struck a boom barrier while en route to Hundru, causing minor damage to the barrier. The security team conducted an alcohol test, which returned negative results. A challan of ?1000 was issued for unsafe driving. A written statement was obtained, and the vehicle was subsequently released.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at ENABLING site at 2025-06-26 11:09
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 11:09 hrs., the control room received information that Tulsi Mahatha and others from Bhagabandh had blocked the gate of Shikhar, protesting the inability of JRL Mining to operate four vehicles. The Security Shift In-Charge and Lead Security Operations reached the site and successfully persuaded them to open the gate. Subsequently, a meeting with Mr. Krishna Jha of JRL Mining commenced at Shikhar Gate. Vehicle movement resumed at 11:32 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at ENABLING site at 23 Jun 2025, 4:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 16:50 hrs., Manoj Mahato, and Raju Mahato, along with 4 to 5 villagers including women, blocked the road at Chamuraidih more due job-related concerns. Upon receiving the information, the Shift In-Charge and QRT team reached the location and attempted to negotiate with the protestors. However, they refused to vacate the road. The matter was reported to the Siyaljori Police, and after police intervention, the road was reopened at 19:45 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Political/ Social at ENABLING site at 27 Jun 2025, 3:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Around 1545 hrs. QRT-3 informed the Security Shift In-Charge that several local villagers from Alkusha had blocked the main road near the railway bridge in Alkusha. The blockade was in response to a cow being fatally struck by an unidentified vehicle. The Chas Mufassil Police Station was informed about the incident. The security team, PR team and CSR team reached the site and talked to the villager’s post discussion and compromise the road blockade was lifted, and normal traffic resumed at 2058 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Govindraj from V. Palya Village blocked the road near the bus stop at 17 Jul 2025, 5:35 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 17.07.2025, at around 0535 hrs, Govindraj from V. Palya Village blocked the road near the bus stop, citing dust pollution as the reason. He claimed that two of his cows had died due to this pollution. The obstruction halted all shift vehicles belonging to Vedanta and JSW, as well as JSW's civil trucks. Following discussions with JSW and Vedanta Security, the issue was temporarily resolved, and the vehicles were released by 0615 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Bommesh, a local job applicant from Bommenahally, blocked shift vehicles at 18 Jul 2025, 8:25 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 18.07.2025, at around 0825 hrs Bommesh, a local job applicant from Bommenahally, blocked shift vehicles, demanding employment. Upon received information, informed to ER/PR for discussion.Meanwhile, the security team quickly intervened, and after a discussion, the situation was temporarily resolved, allowing the vehicles to proceed by 0847 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at ZWM site at 18 Jul 2025, 4:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At 3:35 Information recieved from adit 8 deployed SG Chandra Mohan that thieves movement has been observed nearby adit 8 fan view C2 . As soon as they saw the guards they have started stone pelting from hill side . QRT with ASO and Shift Incharge reached at the location at 4:35 thieves moved away. During heavy stone pelting the tin shade got damaged and they also have broken electrical light at nearby pole. No injury noticed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft at CHP Aneja Batching Plant (TGSPL).
INCIDENTDETAILS: Reported by TGSPL that 30KG of Weing machine has been stolen

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 2025-06-10 07:45
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 07:45 hrs, a trailer loaded with TMT bars, bearing registration number JH02BK 4475 and operated by Singh Enterprises, damaged the boom barrier of Garga Weighbridge No. 02 while reversing the vehicle.  Upon receiving the information, the traffic team promptly reached the spot. An alcohol test was conducted on the driver, which returned negative. The team imposed a penalty of ?1000 for unsafe driving. After the boom barrier was repaired, the vehicle was released at 16:00 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 11 Jun 2025, 12:16 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 0016 hrs, the CCTV operator observed a group of 6 to 7 individuals attempting to enter the premises near the boundary of Tower 17 using a ladder. The security team responded by throwing stones at the intruders, preventing them from entering the plant. The group fled the area around 0130 hrs. Later, at approximately 0200 hrs, another attempt was made to enter the premises near the corner of the oxygen plant. Upon observing the intrusion attempt, the CCTV operator immediately informed the deployed guard and the QRT. The QRT promptly reached the location. Due to the alertness of the security team, all intruders fled the area around 0230 hrs. Upon arrival, the QRT recovered approximately 20 feet of iron scrap pipe which cost approx. Rs 4000, that had been left behind by the fleeing individuals.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ZWM site at 16 Jul 2025, 10:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At 22:50 hrs , SG Padam Bahadur Rana informed on walkie talkie set that he saw movement of 3-4 thieves above SMS Workshop .As soon as they saw security guard chasing them taking the advantage of mountainous height they started stone pelting . Shift incharge , QRT With Aso reached at the location but thieves moved away On site inspection it has been found that the nearby jumbo machine standing in workshop has cable and coupler stolen by thieves. Site CSO reached at the location and inspected the site thoroughly and further investigation is being processed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at VAL J site at 27 Jun 2025, 12:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 0030hrs, a call was received from the security guard deployed at back side of TPP Sepco Barrack, reporting suspicious movement of 4–5 miscreants. The information was immediately conveyed to the SCR. In response, QRT's from IPP, Plant-2 and BOP, along with the Baaz team, promptly reached the location. A thorough area search was conducted, during which a breach in the chain-link fence was discovered, indicating possible unauthorized entry into the plant premises. Although no miscreants were found at the site, approximately 200 kg of scrap material was recovered from outside the boundary wall. The breached section of the fence (fox hole) has now been temporarily repaired.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at RJON-Upstream site at 16 Jun 2025, 9:10 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 16th June 2025, an attempted theft was observed at the S3WH premises by SG Bhawani Singh. A group of local underage children was found involved in attempting to take scrap material from the site.
The scrap material was immediately recovered. The children were identified, and their parents were subsequently informed about the incident.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at RJON-Upstream site at 07 Jul 2025, 5:09 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 07th Jul 2025, at approximately 0505 AM, Security Guard Karna Ram was on duty, and he was on round at CEC Centre, and he heard the voice of Gas pressure leak towards driver bunk, he came back to that side, he watched that someone was trying to theft Air conditioner of driver bunk. During check by security Guard, it is to be noticed that some persons crossed the wall and entered inside the CEC Centre, and they were trying to theft Air Conditioner of driver bunk. As he reached near driver bunk, they crossed the same wall and ran away. Security Guard observed that electric connection and AC Gas pipe disconnected, and AC was disbalanced.  Security Guard reported to FO, AM and ASO Mangla. ASO Mangla reported to Security Manager and Security Manager sent ASO MPT Tirath Singh and Branch Manager RS Security Office to theft attempt site

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 26 Jun 2025, 7:12 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: The control room observed that certain miscreants had entered the plant premises with the apparent intent to commit theft. The information was promptly relayed to the deployed guard and the QRT team. The guard reached the location, and upon noticing his approach, the individuals fled the scene empty-handed leaving scrap material worth Rs 5000/- approx.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 28 Jun 2025, 11:07 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: The control room observed that certain miscreants had entered the plant premises with the apparent intent to commit theft.  The information was promptly relayed to the deployed guard and the QRT team. The guard reached the location, and upon noticing his approach, the individuals fled the scene empty-handed leaving scrap material worth Rs 4000/- Approx.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at ENABLING site at 26 Jun 2025, 8:25 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 0825 hrs., Pradumna Hazara and his family blocked the road near Vedanta Lane Road Nursing Home, demanding employment. The Security team immediately reached the location and tried to convince him to open the block. However, he was adamant. After a lot of convincing attempts, At 1500hrs IR Head, Mukhiya of Alkusha panchayat and Police Chas mufassil held a meeting at Shikhar Gate. and post discussion the road blocked cleared at 1640 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at RDC site at 24 May 2025, 11:40 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: The priest of Bheru temple along with local villagers trespassed and blocked the PCH road due to delay in construction of a room in the temple. Work has not started in the temple for three months. The management team discussed the issue with the villagers and agreed to start the construction work within 15 days. After this, villagers left at 17:15hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Unauthorized entry/ Intrusion/Trespassing at RDC site at 31 May 2025, 12:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: During routine vehicle patrolling approximately 00:30hrs, SG Dammar Raj observed 02 unidentified individuals came inside the PCH-2 scrapyard premises for intention theft scrap. The suspected were fled the scene when guard flashed the torch light. Following the alerts, the QRT was immediately dispatched to the location. 02 individuals were sighted fleeing from the direction of the PCH Scrap Yard. A thorough search of the area was conducted, and no items were found to be missing. No CCTV surveillance is available in the affected area.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at RDC site at 14 May 2025, 5:20 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: M/s MAPL company workers and union members stopped their shift at the Ghati Gate area, demanding the employment of local people. A meeting between union members and the HZL Admin officer addressed this issue, but no solution was found, and the strike continues next day (15/05/2025) they agreed, and shift movement resumed at (15/05/2025)12:30hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at RJON-Upstream site at 12 Jul 2025, 4:20 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On July 12, the security team received information regarding the theft of approximately 10 to 15 feet of earthing cable in MS 01 from beneath the E-House located near the substation at Mangala South 01. Upon inspection, the security personnel conducted a thorough check of the area. During this inspection, footprints were discovered near one corner of the well pad’s perimeter fence, indicating a possible point of unauthorized entry or exit.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at RDC site at 18 Jul 2025, 8:40 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Foield Theft: On 18.07.2025 at around 0840 hrs, Information received from TCL company worker Mr. Bheru Lal about someone damaged his quarter lock. Security team reached and checked the location and found three quarters lock damaged condition. No material was stolen.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at RDC site at 22 Jul 2025, 3:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Foield Theft: On 22/07/2025 at approximately 0300 hrs, information was received at DSC shift ASO from a civilian (Rakesh- Worker of Kesariya Hotel) regarding four thieves stealing HDPE liners from the old SLF area. The QRT and Shift ASO were immediately reached to the location. Upon seeing the approaching security personnel, the thieves abandoned the HDPE liners and fled the scene. All material have been recovered. Material cast approx. 140000 Rs.
LNAME: SLF & Jarofix yard and SS workshop near SLF

INCIDENTTITLE: Bommenahalli blocked shift vehicles at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 09 Jul 2025, 1:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 09.07.2025 at 1330 hrs,villagers in Bommenahalli blocked shift vehicles (BP#4 and 2 Vedansh vehicles) due to poor road conditions. ER/PR department was informed for corrective action. Until the road was repaired, G-Shift return vehicles were diverted to alternative routes. After the repair, the shift vehicles were released at 1735 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at SUVALI (OFFSHORE) site at 20 Jun 2025, 4:21 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Some dry wooden branches were found fallen on the perimeter fencing, posing a potential security and safety risk.

INCIDENTTITLE: All night shift vehicles were stopped at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 13 Jul 2025, 9:35 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 13.07.2025 at around 2135 hrs, all night shift vehicles were stopped in Meghalahalli village by a group of young people. They were demanding the establishment of a gym facility and a Valmiki Samudaya building in their village.The security team engaged in a discussion with the protestors and subsequently informed the ER/PR dept. for further action. Following a discussion led by "Canter Manju," the vehicles were released around 2215 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ZWM site at 11 Jul 2025, 3:35 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: According to the information received from SG Nar Bhahdur Gurung at 03:35 hrs. On July 11, 2025, control, Eagle, Victor, cheetah 2 were informed that there is some activity of thieves in the CBM workshop area, and they have broken the tin roof to take out the jumbo cable and coupler. I (shift IC), Cheetah 2, and Bravo 1 immediately reached the location and saw that the jumbo coupler and cable had been out from backside of the tin roof. Everyone surrounded the workshop area and chased away the thieves, recovering the cable and coupler. A cut mark was found on the cable and the coupler is safe. Three workers were working under Sandvik at the time of the incident, and they didn’t informed the security control room or nearby guard.On asking for their details they refused to shoe gatepass. The on-duty SG Nar bhahdur Gurung was alert,he passed the message to the security team and prevented the theft from happening.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 05 Jun 2025, 4:22 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 04:22 hrs, the CCTV operator observed four women involved in theft at the Sinter Plant. The operator promptly informed the deployed security guard and the Quick Response Team (QRT). Upon receiving the information, the Quick Response Team (QRT) promptly reached the scene and apprehended one of the women involved. The other three women managed to flee the location. The apprehended woman was brought to the security office, where an investigation was conducted. Following the inquiry, a written statement was obtained from her and a family member. After issuing a formal warning, she was released. The stolen items, valued at approximately ?4,000, were successfully recovered by the QRT.

INCIDENTTITLE: Tool Down by G4S/RS Security Drivers (DSC & HZAPL)-10/7/2025
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Date-10.07.2025
 
Today from 0700 hrs all security vehicle drivers of M/s G4S/RS Security deployed at DSC and HZAPL halted security vehicle operations and sitting on a tool down strike,resulting in a total downtime of 59 hours.
 
Reason:
The drivers demanded a revision in their wages,seeking parity with the drivers of Admin vehicle drivers or deployed them inside the plant.
All drivers gathered in the lawn area in front of the logistics office inside the DSC plant and stopped operating security vehicles,insisting that their demand for increase in wages or employment to be  addressed immediately.
After discussions and intervention by Union and management that their demands will be addressed within 15 days of time period. The drivers agreed to resume duties after assurance.
 
Start time :0700 hrs (10/07/25)
End time :1800 hrs (12/07/25)
Total tool down time:59hrs

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 21 Jul 2025, 11:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2300 hrs, the CCTV operator reported that 5 to 6 thieves had entered the 38 Khata Yard 2 area.
The Security Shift IC and QRT team reached the location. As they approached the suspects, the thieves began pelting stones. The team retaliated, causing the suspects to flee beyond the boundary wall. The team remained on standby until 0430 hrs. Fortunately, no injuries were reported due to the stone-pelting. At approximately 0500 hrs, the security team conducted a search of the surrounding area and recovered approximately 300 kg of scrap material (1 piece) which costed RS 10,000/- approx.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 24 Jul 2025, 10:08 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 1008 hrs, CCTV operators observed some woman loitering near the Sierra Post. They appeared to be attempting to steal scrap material from the area.
Due to the alertness of the CCTV operators and the deployed guard, the CRO immediately informed QRT-1 and the Security Shift IC. Upon receiving the information, QRT-1 promptly reached the location. On seeing the QRT team, they fled the scene. Approximately 70 kg of scrap  worth Rs of 2800/- was recovered from the area.

INCIDENTTITLE: Traffic at ENABLING site at 2025-07-25 20:00
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"At around 2000 hrs, the Garga Gate Commander received information regarding vehicle no. OD 04C-2634 (truck), which had delivered materials from the contractor Vesuvius India Limited. While exiting the gate after unloading, the truck damaged the IT cable, leading to a shutdown of the gate's system and CCTV camera. When the guard attempted to stop the vehicle, the driver attempted to flee. The incident has since been reported to the IT department for further action.
At Garga Gate, a challan of ?1000 was issued by the security team for unsafe driving, and a written statement was collected from the driver. Meanwhile, the IT team is replacing the damaged cable. The vehicle has been temporarily halted outside the gate and is being redirected back to the plant. Further action is underway by the security department.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at ENABLING site at 21 Jul 2025, 5:05 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1705 hrs, a vehicle broke down in a ditch due to road damage in front of Hundru Gate. A payloader was dispatched from the plant to recover the vehicle. However, it was stopped by Siyaljori resident Laxman Mahato near 124 Khata. At approximately 1900 hrs, Laxman Mahato family and 3 to 4 other villagers, Joined him and blocked the road.
At 1745 hrs, the security department forcefully removed Laxman Mahato from the road, and the vehicle was successfully pulled out of the ditch. The Siyaljori Police Station In-Charge arrived on time and spoke with Laxman Mahato. The roadblock was finally cleared at 2139 hrs after successful persuasion by the police.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at STEEL site at 2025-07-25 05:55
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 0555 hrs, Dy CSO reported a theft at the SMS site. Preliminary investigation by the shift in-charge revealed that one 25 kg bag of Ferro Vanadium, valued at approximately ?28,000, was missing from the LRF Control Room, where it had been stored in an open drum. Notably, the CCTV inside the room had not been functioning for a long time, and despite having three employees and six workers on each shift handling the material, no one witnessed the theft. The absence was discovered at 0445 hrs when the material was needed. Although there are two exit doors equipped with external CCTV coverage, no immediate clues were available. Further investigation was scheduled to begin after 0900 hrs to gather more information.
The culprit has been identified through CCTV footage.  Although Shift Incharge Ajay Kumar initially raised the complaint and was advised to file an FIR, he stated that further steps will be taken only as per the instructions of the HOD. The security team lodged the complaint.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at ENABLING site at 27 Jul 2025, 3:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:

His mother sustained serious head and mouth injuries and was transported to K.M. Memorial Hospital via tempo, where she was later declared deceased. Following the incident, 50–60 villagers gathered at the scene and assaulted the security guard present.
The police force was duly informed, and all relevant departments have been notified accordingly. The Company management (IR, PR, Security etc.), Villagers and some local politicians went to the Chas Mufassil Police station for negotiations. on 29-07-2025 in Continuation of Fatality Incident of 27th July'25, Negotiations were held between concerned departments and the villagers, finally a settlement was reached. INCIDENTTITLE: Access Related Violations at BALCO site at 2025-07-21 20:10
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 21 July 2025 at approximately 2010 hrs., a tailgating incident was observed at the Parsabhata Pedestrian Exit Gate by the deployed security guard from Plant 3, who immediately informed the CSOC. As a disciplinary measure, Mr. Rohit’s gate pass was confiscated, and the incident was documented and escalated to relevant authorities for further action. As part of the detailed investigation, Mr. Sumit Kumar from M/s Larsen Turbo Limited, supervisor of Mr. Rohit, was also summoned to the location, and his statement was also documented. The incident was reported to all concerned authorities for further necessary action.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at RJON-Upstream site at 22 Jul 2025, 3:24 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 3:00 AM, an attempted theft occurred at the NVU yard of the Bhagyam site. Unknown individuals illegally entered the premises by cutting through the perimeter fence. Upon gaining access, they began loading scrap cables and other materials into two Scorpio vehicles they had brought with them. The on-duty security personnel promptly noticed suspicious activity and immediately alerted the Quick Response Team (QRT) and the site security team. On arriving at the scene, the security teams initiated a pursuit of the intruders. As a result of the chase, the perpetrators abandoned both vehicles and fled the area on foot. The stolen materials, which had already been loaded into the vehicles, were successfully recovered on the spot. The local police were informed without delay. Upon arriving at the scene, the police took both vehicles into custody and initiated further investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at ENABLING site at 08 Jul 2025, 1:10 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 1310 hours, Bankim Chand Mahto, a resident of Siyaljori, along with his family members, blocked the road near the tire shop ahead of Karma Gate. The reason for the roadblock was that Mr. Mahto, who is employed in a BPO, was demanding to be made a regular (on-roll) employee. Additionally, he wanted another member of his family to be employed in the BPO in his place. The movement of pedestrians and two-wheelers continued as usual, but the group blocked the road for four-wheelers.
Upon receiving the information, the Security shift ic reached the spot and attempted to convince them to lift the roadblock, but they refused to comply. The matter was reported to the concerned department. At approximately 2150 hours, the Siyaljori Police In-Charge arrived at the location and also tried to persuade them  to end the protest. However, they again refused. After the discussion, the Police In-Charge left the site. The security team was present throughout the incident. The four-wheeler movement halted, while pedestrian and two-wheeler movement continues. The roadblock was cleared around 1200 hrs on 9th July 2025 with the support of the District Administration. An FIR (NRC NO- 31/2025)  was lodged by the security team against the individuals involved.

INCIDENTTITLE: Fraud/Malpractice at VAL J site at 15 Jan 2026, 6:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1830 hrs, information was received regarding a bribery case at the CH-1 Logistics Yard, Plant-1. Based on the input, the Security Team along with the Baaz Team immediately reached the location and caught Mr. Bipin Kumar Sahoo, OSL Supervisor (Gate Pass No. 6600017654/10/00088, Orissa Stevedores Ltd.), red-handed while accepting money from FG vehicle drivers for loading purposes inside the logistics yard. During preliminary questioning and detailed interrogation, Mr. Bipin Kumar Sahoo voluntarily admitted his involvement in the bribery. The bribe amount( rs 200) was recovered from his possession during frisking, which he had received from an FG vehicle driver. Further, during his written and video statements, Mr. Bipin Kumar Sahoo disclosed that this bribery activity involves multiple individuals, including Supervisors and workers of OSL Company Hydra operators deployed at the location Workers and supervisors of Krishca Strapping Solutions Limited, the agency responsible for tarpaulin covering The Security Team has collected substantial and valid evidence in connection with this case, including: Recovery of bribe money Written statement of the accused Video statement of the accused. Mail was done to concern team for further action.
RCA-"1.  Lack of continuous monitoring and surprise checks at the logistics yard allowed unethical practices to go unnoticed over time.
2   Absence of strict access and transaction control mechanisms during loading activities enabled individuals to influence the process unofficially.
3  High dependency on contract manpower and multiple agencies at the same operational point created gaps in accountability and supervision"

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ZWM site at 13 Aug 2025, 5:23 PM

On 13/08/2025 at 1723hrs

When : 13 August 2025
 Where : Near Shiv Temple , Balaria mines 
Who : Three unidentified miscreants 
Why : Copper cable lying in cable yard with very low fencing height & no fencing in backside .
How : The miscreanyts approached from backside from hilly terrain , cut & stolen cable valued around 3k .

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at ENABLING site at 09 Aug 2025, 7:20 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 8th Aug 2025, at around 07:20 hrs, a group of 25 to 30 individuals from Ansari Tola village gathered at the gate and demanded that it be opened. After some time they leaved the place. Post this On 9th Aug 2025, at around 09:03 hrs, during CCTV surveillance, movement of several villagers was observed. Within approximately 10 minutes, a crowd of 70 to 80 individuals had gathered, armed with sticks, stones, iron rods, and crowbars. At 09:22 hrs, the villagers from Ansari Tola broke the lock of the Ansari Gate using sticks. The entire crowd then assembled near the mosque area. After that at around 18:40 hrs, CCTV operators observed police movement at Ansari Gate. Aalam Ansari, along with 20 to 30 people, was seen engaging in a discussion with the police team. After the conversation with the villagers, the patrolling team departed the location at around 18:50 hrs. After that on 13th Aug 2025, At around 1125 hrs, approximately 25 to 30 residents of Ansari Tola village gathered near the Maithan office regarding concerns over the opening and closing of the Ansari Tola gate. 
Security present at the site and information regarding this passed to concerned department. This information was promptly reported to the Security Officer and the Siyaljori Police Administration. The security team remained on high alert at Ansari Gate. The situation at the gate remained normal, and the gate continued to remain open. The QRT team also conducted continuous patrolling in the area. however the incident on 9th Aug the Complaint was made with police station against the villagers involved in the inchident. Post this on 13th Aug a meeting was held between the villagers and the CSR department at the Maithan conference hall. Dy. CSO and Siyaljori Police were also present during the meeting with the villagers. The meeting concluded inconclusively at 1310 hrs. The security team remained present at the location to manage any untoward incidents.

NSP Security Incharge promptly informed Dy. CSO-IOG and Mines Manager.
Dy. CEO, Mr. Dhiraj Kumar Jagdish, along with Mines Manager, Mr. Laxman Gawas, and Safety Officer, Mr. Mukilan K., immediately visited the incident location for assessment.

INCIDENTTITLE: Group of locals are stopped B shift vehicles in Konanur cross at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 05 Sep 2025, 1:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"On 05.09.2025, at around  1330 hrs, a group of locals, including Shiva Prasad and Rajanna, blocked B- shift vehicles at Konanur Cross. They alleged that the vehicles were traveling at excessive speeds through their village routes.
Security personnel were alerted and promptly informed the administration, ER/PR, and the Seregire police. A phone call was held between security, the police, and the locals. However, the villagers refused to release the vehicles, insisting they would not move them without a formal permission letter for route passage. They also demanded to speak directly with an HR/Admin representative and the bus owners.
The situation was temporarily resolved after the Seregire police arrived at the scene. A joint discussion with the police, ER/PR, and security led to the release of the vehicles at 1550 hrs.
The locals raised three specific demands:
 Speed Control: They want shift vehicles to maintain a controlled speed on village roads.
 Road Maintenance: They are demanding the cleaning of vegetation/bushes from both sides of the roads.
 Dust Compensation: The locals are seeking compensation for dust pollution caused by the vehicles."

INCIDENTTITLE: PR-Villagers/Neighbourhood at ENABLING site at 07 Aug 2025, 11:25 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: The installation of fencing pillars along the railway line on the Vedanta lane road was being carried out under the supervision of the Project Department. However, Mahadev Hazara, son of Sanjay Hazara, along with his family from Dhanadabar village, halted the work due to issues related to non-payment for land and compensation. As a result, all workers and machinery were withdrawn from the site. The project head and the QRT team were present at the location during the incident.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR - Worker/ Union/ Transporters at ENABLING site at 14 Aug 2025, 2:00 PM

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 12 Aug 2025, 10:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2245 hrs, QRT 4 received information that a group of 10 to 12 miscreants had arrived at 38 Khata Yard and were engaged in stone pelting.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at VAB site at 30 Apr 2025, 1:25 PM

Their vehicle had arrived three days earlier and remained parked outside the gate due to pending gate pass formalities required for unloading fines material. INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 2025-08-15 23:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
8 to 10 individuals entered the Upper Yard of the Garga area by jumping over the boundary wall, with the intention of theft and began pelting stones at the security personnel. 
As the situation escalated, the QRT, Security Shift In-Charge, and on-duty guard were immediately alerted. The security team promptly arrived at the scene and attempted to disperse the intruders. In retaliation, the thieves began pelting stones at the security personnel. The stone-pelting continued for approximately one hour, after which the security team successfully drove all the intruders away from the area.

INCIDENTTITLE: THEFT
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 07th Aug 2025 at around 1705 hrs., 03 workers of sub vendor M/s Trident Fabricators under M/s ABB India Limited. namely 1. Sonu Ratnakar -5600001782/20/00511, 2. Bishi Keshan Kishan-5600001782/20/00518, 3. Subodh Chhatar – 5600001782/20/00519 detected suspicious activities inside their temporary store near the AHP tarpaulin point CPP. During the security check, it was found that they were cutting and pilling some scrap copper cables (approx. 200 gm) and aluminum cable (approx. 350 gm) with the intention of stealing. "On 18th Aug’25 information was received by the Security Control Room that a person named Alam Ansari, resident of Ansari Tola, had stopped the ongoing work related to the Railway Project near Shikhar Gate. The reason cited was an issue related to employment.
Again, on 21st Aug’25 Alam Ansari had stopped the railway work at Shikhar gate."

On 21st The IR head and Security head convinced him and work started after an hour."

INCIDENTTITLE: IR - Worker/ Union/ Transporters at ENABLING site at 01 Aug 2025, 11:20 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 1120 hours, around 35 to 40 workers from Singh Enterprises, working under DIP, gathered at the IR office. They were protesting against the contractor, alleging that they had not been provided with 26 days of work. Following the discussion, the workers dispersed and returned to the DIP area at around 1345 hours. During the entire episode, the security team remained on high alert at the site to prevent any untoward incidents.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 10 Aug 2025, 10:09 AM

INCIDENTTITLE: Dharna at CLZS(CHANDERIYA) site at 09 Sep 2025, 2:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
A group of approximately 70–80 workers from A & B shift assembled at the Runaya plant gate and initiated a tool-down strike. They demanded discussion with Runaya Management regarding safe practices in the operational area. There was a heavy sound incident occurred last night on the shopfloor, which terrified the workers.

Further, they added more demands including double salary on PH, uniform, bonus, PPEs, salary increment etc.  Runaya Management has already addressed all these demands except for the uniform which also in pipeline.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at RJON-Upstream site at 12 Aug 2025, 7:04 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
It is reported that unknown persons have committed theft at the operation camp site located in Mangla Field after site fence cut. The following items were stolen during the incident:
1.	Micro Sensor with SIM Serial Number: 8991000923271091899U / SIM Number: 9257039457
2.	Solar Plate with Batteries
The theft was carried out by unidentified individuals, and the matter was immediately noted upon inspection of the site by caretaker Rama ram. The loss has been recorded and reported for necessary action and investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 17 Aug 2025, 6:11 AM

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at ENABLING site at 20 Aug 2025, 5:50 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"At Around 0550 hrs - 20-25 people including women under the banner of ""Jharkhand Shoshan Mukti Ulgulan Manch"", gathered at Alkusha More and blocked all vehicle movement due to land issue.  All A-Shift buses entered the plant on time.
0810 hrs - When the police team tried to convince the protesters, they turned aggressive and attacked the police with stones and sticks.  During this incident, the SHO of Siyaljori sustained a head injury and was immediately shifted to the hospital.
1015 hrs - A reinforcement of about 50-60 police personnel reached the site, conducted a lathi charge, arrested all protesters and cleared the road block.     "
"All Security personnel reported in early morning and adequate strength was deployed at the site.  Police were present at the location and the protest was peaceful initially.  
Police and Security teams remain deployed at the location to monitor and tackle any further situation.  "

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 25 Jan 2026, 10:54 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 25th January 2026 at around 22:54hrs, a CCTV alert was notified that some thieves were removing material from behind the CH2 area. The thieves abandoned the material and fled when the guards reached the site to apprehend them. 15 aluminum ingots recovered approximately costing Rs. 21,700/- during search in the area near the railway track behind CH2. It was also observed that the sheet on backside of CH2 had been bent, and the boundary fencing near Samadhi area was found cut, which was used by the thieves for perching inside the premises. Again, at 00:04hrs on 26th January 2026, the thieves approached again, as informed by the CSOC. NDO Mr. Durgesh Singh and PS Arun Singh reached the site promptly and successfully apprehended two thieves, namely Nikhil Singh (16 yrs) and Aman Singh (17 yrs). An FIR has been lodged with BALCO police, and both the apprehended personnel were handed over to them for further legal actions.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at IRON ORE GOA site at 28 Jan 2026, 2:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Mr. Samir Chauhan and Mr. Vishnu Morajkar, residents of the Pilgao area, unlawfully stopped our iron ore transportation trucks at Mathwada Junction, stating that the road condition was not good. However, in reality, the road condition was satisfactory and there was no dust emission on the said road.
Due to their actions, our iron ore transportation activity was unnecessarily disrupted and stopped for nearly one hour, from 1450 hrs to 1550 hrs, causing operational delays and losses. Immediately informed to Police and the transport was resumed at 1550hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2026-01-15 15:00
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 15th January 2026 at around 15:00 hours, an intelligence alert reported the suspected involvement of Mr. Anand Kumar Keshari (Unique ID: 220149), a Consultant from M/s Bulk MRO Industrial Supply working in Potline-2 (G Shift), in the unauthorized removal of seven crane sensors and tools concealed in his personal bag. Upon receiving the alert, CSOC immediately informed the Plant-1/3 In-Charge, and security personnel at Parsabhata Gate and Alumina Gate were placed on heightened vigilance. During shift-out at Alumina Gate, the Security Team intercepted Mr. Keshari and, during detailed frisking, recovered the seven crane sensors and associated tools valued at approximately Rs. 8,000/-. His preliminary statement was recorded, and his gate pass, along with those of other involved personnel, was confiscated for verification. The recovered materials and gate pass were seized and securely deposited in the Plant-1 Security Store, and the matter has been escalated to the HR Department for further action as per company policy.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at BALCO site at 2026-01-15 23:30
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 15th January 2026 at around 23:30 hours, the CSOC detected three unidentified individuals breaching the perimeter wall near APP-IN, Plant-1 during routine CCTV monitoring. The intrusion was immediately escalated to the NDO Officer and Plant-1/2 In-Charge, following which the Quick Response Team (QRT) was promptly dispatched. Security personnel conducted a thorough search of the area, but the intruders fled upon noticing the approaching team and took no material. The situation was brought under control, and enhanced surveillance measures were initiated to monitor further suspicious activity.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 17 Jan 2026, 6:06 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 17th January 2026, at approximately 06:06 hours, an intelligence alert was received regarding the suspected involvement of Mr. Dipak Mahant and Mr. Vikram Bhatt, employees of M/s Larsen & Toubro Private Limited working at the New Bake Oven, in the unauthorized removal of four bundles of copper wire concealed inside a personal bag. Upon receiving the alert, the Central Security Operations Centre (CSOC) immediately informed the Plant 1/3 In-Charge, and security personnel at Parsabhata Gate and Alumina Gate were placed on heightened vigilance. During shift-out at Alumina Gate, the Security Team conducted targeted frisking and intercepted both individuals, who subsequently abandoned the concealed material at the gate and fled. A preliminary statement was obtained from the concerned supervisor, and their gate passes were confiscated. Detailed frisking resulted in the recovery of four bundles of copper wire valued at Rs. 8,832/-, and the seized material along with the gate passes was secured in the Plant-1 Security Store. Statements from associated personnel were recorded, and the matter has been escalated to the HR Department for further action as per company policy.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 26 Feb 2026, 12:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
This is to inform you that at around 1245 hours, input was received from internal intelligence regarding suspicious movement of an ash vehicle bearing registration number OD23B1147. Based on the intelligence input, the vehicle was immediately intercepted and detained at Bhagipali Gate by the Security and BAAZ team. The vehicle was unloaded near the gate premises for detailed inspection. During thorough checking, a significant quantity of courtyard iron-type scrap, aluminum dross, and green anode material was recovered, which had been deliberately concealed under ash material to avoid detection. The vehicle has been detained, and further investigation is currently in progress.
RCA-"1. Weak Access & Key Management Controls
•Courtyard 1 key was issued based on a internal soil shifting without verification or dual authorization.
•Absence of strict key control SOPs enabled unauthorized entry into a sensitive material zone.
2. Inadequate Supervision & Monitoring
•Loading operations in Courtyard 1 were not supervised, allowing concealed loading under soil.
•No verification of soil shifting activity vs. actual work location.
3. CCTV & Surveillance Gaps
•Critical blind spots in Courtyard 1 allowed theft activities to occur without visual detection.
•No CCTV supported cross verification of JCB and Hywa operations.
5. Lack of Contractor Workforce Governance
•No risk based monitoring of contractor staff frequently accessing sensitive areas.
•Multi person collusion (Asha Movers + TIS) remained undetected over multiple attempts.
6. Financial Incentives & External Linkage
•?4,000 transactions demonstrate monetary collusion and incentive-driven participation.
•External receiver (Pradeep Rohidas) facilitated disposal, enabling continued theft cycles.
7. Non Implementation of Security Recommendations
•Previously reported surveillance and process loopholes were not addressed, enabling repetition of the same modus operandi."

INCIDENTTITLE: Property Related at BALCO site at 2025-08-15 04:32
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 15.08.2025, at approximately 0432 hrs., Mr. A.P. Chatterjee, 540 Shift IC reported a suspected theft attempt in the Unit 3 Turbine 5 Meter Panel Room. The Plant 2 In-charge and NDO promptly reached the location for investigation.An unidentified worker, dressed in a red uniform, had entered the panel room through the emergency gate. The individual was attempting to cut the earth trip berth bar (weighing 5.3 kg) value Rs. 3500/- of Unit 3 ACDB Live DHP Pump Unit 3 A by removing two knots each. Field Engineer Mr. Bhupendra, who was patrolling nearby, heard the sound of a grinder machine and rushed to the scene. Upon noticing him, the unknown person fled the location empty-handed. Mr. Bhupendra was unable to see the individual’s face, hence the suspect could not be identified. The grinder machine used was identified as belonging to AHP 540 MW, Equipment ID: A-G-4-2. The matter was immediately reported to AHP Shift InCharge Mr. Deepak Yadav for further action. The material targeted in the theft attempt has been successfully recovered and secured.

INCIDENTTITLE: shift vehicles from Chitradurga were stopped at Bommenahalli by Venkatesh at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 23 Sep 2025, 1:10 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: shift vehicles from Chitradurga were stopped at Bommenahalli by Venkatesh, who demanded a job. Bouncers were diverted to the location, and the Hireguntanuru police were informed for support. After the police arrived and held discussions, the issue was temporarily resolved, and the shift vehicles were released at 1355 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2025-08-22 17:40
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 22nd August 2025, at approximately 1740 hours, a pilferage attempt was detected through CCTV surveillance at Parsabhata Gate. Mr. Onkar Sharan Singh (UID: 60000000012), an employee of M/s Shree Krishna Engineering currently assigned to the HMA Canteen, was observed carrying one company-owned grinding wheel valued at ?100 while attempting to exit the premises. Immediate investigation revealed that Mr. Singh had failed to deposit the grinding wheel in the store before leaving the workplace. The item was recovered and seized on the spot. Statement from Mr. Singh has been recorded and matter was escalated to HR for further investigation.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR - Worker/ Union/ Transporters at BALCO site at 2025-08-23 11:30
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 23rd August 2025, at approximately 1130 hours, an incident was reported by Project Infra team regarding  scuffle  at Potline 3, Section 8. During a site inspection, Mr. Yellapu Narayana (Engineer, Beauro Veritas, Site Inspection at PL-3) had a verbal disagreement with Mr. Arindam Ghosh and Mr. Brijendar Maity, both L&T Quality and Execution Engineers. Following this exchange, L&T subcontracting supervisor Mr. Pradeep Singh attempted to physically confront Mr. Narayana.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2025-09-02 04:11
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 02 September 2025, at approx. 0411hrs., during CCTV Surveillance Centralized Security Operation center reported  a theft attempt by two miscreants near the old Wine Shop boundary wall P-33 & 34 Plant 2. The nearest deployed security guard, along with the Plant 2 Shift In-charge and NDO officer, immediately responded and carried out a thorough search of the area. During the ambush, the security team apprehended one miscreant and recovered stolen material Hollow Cylindrical pipes, Metal Hammer, Iron Plate & Project Material_310 Kgs, valued at ?6200/- from the location. The apprehended individual, identified as a minor, was handed over to the local police, who later released him with a warning.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2025-09-05 22:40
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 5th September 2025 at approx. 2240 hrs., an intelligence alert was received regarding the suspected involvement of Mr. Rahul Rajput (UID No.: 50000003827), a supervisor from M/s NTPC GE Power Services under subcontract M/s N M Enterprises, in the pilferage of copper bearings concealed in his bag. The Central Security Operations Center (CSOC) immediately informed the Plant 1 and Plant 3 In-Charge and placed security personnel at Parsabhata and Alumina Gates on high alert. A coordinated frisking operation during the C Shift at Parsabhata Gate led to Mr. Rajput’s interception while allegedly attempting to remove copper material from the premises. Upon apprehension, his gate pass was confiscated, and a search recovered five copper pieces weighing 1.620 kg, valued at ?1400. Mr. Rajput admitted to knowingly placing the items in his bag with intent to remove them, violating company policy. His return statement was recorded, and the recovered items and gate pass were seized. His area in-charge, Mr. Rajesh Kumar Mehto (UID: 50000003812), was also summoned and his statement documented. The case has been escalated to the HR department for further action as per company policy.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 10 Sep 2025, 2:15 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 10th September 2025 at approx. 14:15 hrs., during regular frisking, security guard deployed at Parsabhata 2w Out Gate caught Mr. Sudhakar Kumar Kharawar from M/s NTPC GE Power Services Sub-contractor: M/s SSS KA JV who was carrying a small brass rod with value ?150/- in his vehicle. His entry permit was immediately seized, and BALCO HR, along with other relevant stakeholders, was promptly informed for necessary action. The HR representative of M/s NTPC GE Power Services and Mr. Sudhakar’s reporting officer were called to the gate for further review. Written statements were obtained from Mr. Sudhakar, his HR representative Mr. Vinit Pandey (UID: 1539381), and his operations in-charge Mr. Praveen Kumar (UID: 1566313). Mr. Sudhakar’s gate pass was confiscated to restrict further access, and the matter has been escalated to the concerned HR department for appropriate action as per company policy.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2025-09-12 18:29
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 12th September 2025 at approximately 18:29 hrs., an intelligence input regarding a suspected pilferage attempt involving vehicle number CG05AR6620. The vehicle was intercepted at the Alumina 4-Wheeler Out Gate and subjected to a frisking check, during which a new bundle of copper cable (300 meters in length, valued at ?11,330) was discovered concealed under the driver’s seat. A detailed investigation was immediately initiated in the presence of the Security Team, revealing that Md. Saddam Alam (UID: 1709229), Electrical Incharge, and Md. Sohel Khan (UID: 1711540), Driver—both employees of M/s Awadh Engineering Pvt. Ltd—had attempted to remove the material from the plant without proper authorization. Their gate passes were confiscated, and written statements were recorded from both individuals, as well as from HR representative Mr. Vinit Pandey (UID: 1539381) and operations in-charge Mr. Praveen Kumar (UID: 1566313). The matter was escalated to the concerned HR department for further action as per company policy, and the recovered material was securely stored in the Plant-1 Security Store.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 22 Sep 2025, 2:25 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 1425hrs, during routine check of vehicles at SEZ exit, an aluminum welding wire bundle was recovered from Bus OD23N4777 of Shree Krishna Enterprises driven by Manoj Biswal. Preliminary investigation by the SEZ team, it was revealed that the material originated from the contractor store while a valid inward store entry was found and no store material exit process has been recorded. Further scrutiny has revealed multiple regular transactions linked to Store In-charge SK Salauddin (GP No-660000/6245/580/0012). The incident reflects a potential breach in material movement control.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Political/ Social at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2025-10-13 09:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 13.10.2025, at approximately 0930 hrs, team from News Channel Vijay Times arrived at the Y-Junction and requested access to the JSW area.
The security team initially granted access by lifting the boom barrier for going towards JSW Mines. However, as the news team proceeded and turned toward the BBH siding, security personnel quickly intervened, stopping them near the MEL weighbridge. The media team, along with several local residents, was ultimately prevented from proceeding further and returned from the Y-Junction.
Local Persons Accompanying the Media
The news team was accompanied by five local individuals:
 1)Ramesh, B Durga
 2)  Devraj, Chitradurga
 3) Sunil ,V Palya
 4) Thimmesh, V Palya
 5) Viresh, Bheemasamudra

Concerns and Allegations:-
The news team and residents were focused on several key issues in the area:
 Blocked Drainage: The obstruction or covering of an old forest stream (nala) that leads to the siding.
 Road Conditions: The poor state of the road in the V Palya Village area.
 Vehicle Overloading: Concerns about excessive loading of vehicles.
  Lack of Tarpaulin Covers: Vehicles moving toward the BBH Railway Siding were not covered with tarpaulin.
 Environmental and Health Impact: Issues related to dust, public health, and damage to forest vegetation.
Subsequent Action:-
The group has threatened to file a formal complaint with the Lokayukta, claiming that a site visit by the Lokayukta will take place within the next seven days. Following the denial of access, the Vijay Times team and the locals proceeded to the Sasalu Railway Siding to film and document the vehicle movements along that route.

INCIDENTTITLE: Organized theft in collusion with insider at CCP site at 09 Aug 2025, 11:54 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 09/08/2025 at around 3:30 pm, we received information that on 08/08/2025 at approximately 9:30 pm, Mr. Krutisunder Mohapatra (Quarter No. C6) had locked his house and gone out. When he returned on 09/08/2025 at 3:30 pm, he found that the lock of his house was broken, and the house had been ransacked. Upon inspection, he discovered that approximately INR 200,000 in cash was missing.
It is suspected that the theft took place between 9:30 pm on 08/08/2025 and 3:30 pm on 09/08/2025 during his absence. The victim has mentioned suspicion on a maid who has been working in his house for the past eight months.

INCIDENTTITLE: Affected Areas Struggle Committee to the Vedanta Mines on 04 November 2025., 2:25 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
This is to report the visit of  the Karnataka Mine Affected Areas Struggle Committee to the Vedanta Mines on 04 November 2025.
At approximately 1425 hrs, a team led by:
•	Mr. Rameshappa – Founder President
•	Mr. Ahimsa Chetan – Honorary President
•	Mr. Radhakrishna Pallakki – State President
along with other members, arrived at the Y-Junction and requested access to the mining area of Vedanta.
The deployed security team at Y-Junction denied the access promptly informed all concerned departments. Upon receiving permission from the Forest Department, the team was escorted from Y-Junction to Gate #2 at around 1500 hrs, accompanied by Police personnel, Forest officers, and Vedanta staff.
Upon reaching the gate #2 main gate which was locked, the delegation inquired about mining activities during night hours. They sought permission to enter the mines, which was denied by Vedanta mangement. Subsequently, they returned to Y-Junction at approximately 1540 hrs and proceeded towards B.Durga via King Gate.
Later, at around 1630 hrs, the team arrived at Gate #1 and again requested access to the mines. The security team denied entry, and the delegation departed the location at approximately 1638 hrs.

In his statement, Mr. Tij Ram claimed he believed his punch-out was successful. Written statement obtained. Gate Pass seized.

RCA- Failure to follow proper exit protocol, resulting in unauthorized tailgating.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at VAL J site at 12 Nov 2025, 5:56 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 0556 hrs. At Fortune Gate A-Shift in-time checking,  observed that employee Shailesh Singh (Gate Pass No. 6600016760/160/00157), Designation: Supervisor, Company: PAPL, had tailgated another person identified as Bikash Kumar Yadav (6600016760/160/0015) Company: PAPL. Action Taken: Both gate passes were confiscated. 

RCA- Tailgating happened due to poor monitoring at the turnstile and failure to enforce strict one-pass-per-person entry rules.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 21 Mar 2026, 4:20 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 21st March 2026 at 16:20 hrs, intelligence input was received regarding suspicious activity at Potline-1, Section-2. The Intelligence Team was immediately alerted and reached the location immediately. The team closely monitored the suspect’s activity and observed an individual attempting to cut a copper cable. The Intelligence Team took immediate action and caught the individual red-handed along with the stolen material. Information about the theft incident was immediately shared with all concerned authorities. Upon receiving the information, Plant-02 S/IC, along with the Quick Reaction Team (QRT), reached the site and carried out a detailed investigation. During the investigation, it was found that Mr. Sanker Srivas (UID No. 10700), an employee of M/s Raju Engineering, was involved in the theft attempt. The accused was attempting to steal approximately 45 kg of copper cable, valued at ?33,120/-, belonging to RMPL contractor material. During the search, one steel knife was recovered from the possession of the accused. As part of consequential management, the statement of the accused was recorded. The accused was thereafter handed over to BALCO Police Station for further legal action. An FIR was jointly lodged by RMPL and the Security Team for investigation and necessary action.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 26 Mar 2026, 4:20 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 26th March 2026, at 16:20 hrs, a pilferage attempt was detected due to an intelligence alert reported, at the project gate. A soil-loaded HYWA vehicle CG12BK0254, belonging to M/s Miraj Developers (subcontractor of Mahamaya) and driven by Mr. Rameshwar Das (UID: 80000020481), was stopped for inspection due to the intel reported. During the checking process, 05 Poclain teeth, valued at Rs. 7000/- were found concealed inside the vehicle, being taken out of the premises without an NRGP and without the knowledge or approval of the concerned department. An investigation was immediately initiated. It was found that Mr. Santosh Kumar (UID: 80000019474), a Poclain Operator from M/s Miraj Developers, had instructed Mr. Rameshwar Das to transport the material out of the premises. Following this discovery, the gate passes of both individuals were confiscated, and their statements were recorded. A statement was also taken from their site supervisor for further clarification.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 27 Mar 2026, 9:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 27th March 2026 at 21:00 hrs., based on intelligence inputs, an attempted theft was detected at the 1200 MW Cooling Tower, where Mr. G. Sonu, identified as an outsider, was found attempting to remove an actuator. The security team promptly reached the spot, apprehended him red-handed, and recovered approximately 70 kg of actuator valued at around ?30,000. A preliminary investigation was done, following the writing of the statement. The individual, along with the recovered material, was handed over to the Police, and an FIR No. 0178 was subsequently lodged at the BALCO Police Station.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 28 Mar 2026, 5:42 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 28th March 2026 at 17:42 hrs, during routine CCTV surveillance, Mr. Gokul Sakia observed suspicious activity at the project gate. The information was immediately communicated to Mr. Anuj Bara, Plant-1 In-Charge, along with other concerned personnel. A response team promptly reached the location and conducted a thorough inspection. During the inspection, Mr. Jitendra Kumar (UID: 80000002072), a worker of M/s Associated Industrial Furnaces, was apprehended while attempting to exit the premises with four (04) cable glands concealed inside his helmet. Subsequently, written statements were obtained from the concerned worker as well as the AIFL contractor service representative, who confirmed that the material had been taken from the CH-4 area. Additionally, the supervisor’s statement was also recorded for further verification. The incident has been documented and reported for further review and necessary action as per company procedures.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 30 Mar 2026, 2:20 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 30th March 2026, at around 14:20 hours, an intelligence input was received regarding the suspected involvement of Mr. Ghanshyam Lal Patel, an employee of M/s Raju Engineering deployed at GAP work as a painter. The information suggested that he had allegedly attempted to remove four bundles of copper wire by concealing them in a personal bag without official authorization. Based on this input, the CSOC promptly informed the Plant-1 and Plant 3 incharges. Security guards posted at Parsabhata Gate and Alumina Gate were alerted and instructed to remain vigilant. During the A-Shift exit process at Parsabhata Gate, the security team carried out focused frisking and intercepted Mr. Ghanshyam Lal Patel while he was attempting to leave the premises with concealed company material. During the inspection, copper wire weighing approximately 3.5 kg, cut into pieces and hidden inside his lunch bag, was recovered. Upon questioning, the individual acknowledged that the material had been collected from the rodding area during morning hours and that he intended to remove it from the plant premises during shift exit. A written statement was recorded from Mr. Ghanshyam, and his gate pass was seized. Further verification confirmed the recovery of four bundles of copper wire weighing 3.5 kg, with an estimated value of ?2,576. The recovered material and the gate pass were taken into security custody. The incident was escalated to the HR Department for further necessary action.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR - Worker/ Union/ Transporters at ENABLING site at 17 Dec 2025, 5:45 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 0545 hrs, around 20–25 workers from different vendors blocked Vedanta Road near Gidhtand, halting shift buses. The workers raised demands concerning wages, change of contractor, and other related issues. They also expressed unwillingness to register in the ACS system and undergo the Annual PME. The Grievance Committee team promptly engaged with the workers, and a joint meeting was convened at Shikhar Gate, conference hall at around 1220 hrs involving the workers, Grievance Committee members, CHRO, Labour Commissioner, Police, and Administration. Following detailed discussions, the road blockade was cleared at approximately 15:00 hrs. The situation was effectively brought under control through coordinated efforts, and production remained unaffected. The security team was present throughout the discussions to ensure order and safety.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at ENABLING site at 22 Dec 2025, 5:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 21.12.2025, Deep Chand Rajak, a worker of TPL Services Private Limited (Gate Pass No. 74301), assigned at BF, completed his A Shift duty and exited the plant at 14:00 hrs. Later, at approximately 19:30 hrs, he was found unconscious and critically injured near PS Bangaria, located about 12–14 km from the plant premises. He was immediately taken by police to KM Memorial Hospital, where he was declared dead. Following the incident, on 22.12.2025, at around 17:45 hrs, a group of 15–20 members from JLKM/BKMS (political parties), along with the deceased’s family members, assembled at Shikhar Gate, blocked the road, and demanded compensation for the deceased’s family.  Discussions and negotiations with TPL management, involving the deceased’s family members, were conducted at the Shikhar Gate conference room in the presence of the local administration. After detailed deliberations and successful negotiations, the road blockade was cleared at approximately 22:10 hrs. Security personnel and the police team remained present throughout the discussions to ensure order and safety.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at ENABLING site at 29 Dec 2025, 7:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 0730 hrs, QRT-03 informed the Shift IC that a trailer was being searched by local individuals from Hazra Tola at the chimney parking area. The trailer had reportedly taken a shortcut route from Bandhdih through Hazra Tola at around 0230 hrs. As per the driver’s statement, the vehicle struck and damaged an 11,000-volt electric pole inside the village. At the time of the incident, two individuals—Chiru Mahto of Ashanbani Maduniya and another person, were also crossing the road and sustained injuries. Both injured persons were shifted to BGH Hospital by local villagers. At approximately 1630 hrs, local villagers blocked the Vedanta four-lane near the chimney parking area in protest over an electricity issue, following damage to an electric pole caused by a vehicle. Upon receiving the information, QRT reached the location and collected details regarding the incident and the vehicle. The particulars are as follows: Vehicle No.: NL-01 AJ 9306, Driver: Dilip Kumar Yadav and Transporter: C J Darcl Transport. All vehicle documents and the key have been secured by QRT-03. Upon receiving the information, QRT reached the location and engaged with the villagers to resolve the situation. The roadblock was cleared by 1807 hrs after the vehicle owner, whose vehicle had damaged the electric pole, paid Rs. 16,000 as compensation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at VLCTPP site at 16 Jan 2026, 6:34 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
•	At approximately 02:25 PM, a message was received from Mr. Shanmukh (PMPL) informing that a person was found hanging by the neck inside a labour hutment at VLCTPP.
•	The QRT/Security team was immediately mobilized to the location, and the area was cordoned off to prevent any disturbance.
•	The incident was reported to TI, Dabhara Police Station at 02:35 PM.
•	The police team arrived at the site at around 03:30 PM and initiated investigation and other legal formalities.
•	At approximately 04:00 PM, after completion of necessary legal procedures, the hutment door lock was broken by the police.
•	Around 04:30 PM, the body was shifted to Dabhara Government Hospital.
•	At approximately 05:00 PM, the body was moved to the mortuary for post-mortem examination.
•	The post-mortem was completed around 06:30 PM, after which the body was handed over to the relatives of deceased.
•	At around 07:00 PM, the deceased’s body was dispatched to his native place.
•	On the next day i.e. 17.01.2026 at approximately 09:00 AM, the body was reached to the native village in Sonbhadra, Uttar Pradesh.
•	The last rites were performed on the same day (17.01.2026) by the family members.
•	On 17.01.2026 Puja/Hawan also performed in S-9 at labour hutment. 
Report on the Suicidal case- Deceased Details:
Name of Deceased: Kamlesh s/o Rambharos

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 29 Jan 2026, 6:40 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1840 hrs, a theft incident was observed at the backside near the gate of the Dross Yard, Runaya Project. One miscreant entered the premises by jumping over the boundary wall and was seen fleeing outside the boundary carrying approximately 20–25 kg of iron forklift fork scrap. The activity was noticed by a Runaya employee who immediately informed the security team. The security team responded promptly along with the Baaz team, and a search operation was conducted outside the boundary. During the search, approximately 40 meters of fiber cable was recovered from outside the boundary area. It was also observed that Runaya scrap material had been dumped near the boundary wall. This issue had already been communicated earlier via email, and instructions were once again issued for the immediate removal of the scrap material. An ambush was planned, but no movement was observed, and boundary gaps were temporarily closed with GI sheets.
RCA-"1. The primary root cause of the incident was the intentional and unauthorized attempt by the miscreant to remove company scrap material.
2. Absence of strict scrap accounting, segregation, and immediate disposal mechanisms at the generation point increased the opportunity for theft
3. As height of boundary wall is low in the area and nearby tree outside boundary wall helped intruder/miscreant to take access easily inside plant
4. Lack of monitoring and supervision in the area makes it a low deterrence zone and more approachable area by miscreant
5. Non deployment of security personnel for a long time increased risk of theft."
LNAME: Plant-1 Runaya Project Site( Dross Area)

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 02 Feb 2026, 7:38 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1938 hrs, a call was received regarding a man entering the A-09 scrap yard by jumping the perimeter from the diesel bunk area. Guard in the adjoining area spotted this suspicious activity and informed SCR. Plant 1 QRT reached on spot and conducted a thorough search, and the suspect attempted to flee. He was chased and apprehended at the VMS office. The individual was identified as Rohit Kumar Pandey, Camper Driver, HenconCompany (Gate Pass No.3402001158/20/00083) who during investigation confessed that he intended to pilfer scrap for selling outside and was running away due to fear of being caught by Security on being spotted. The HenconHOD and area in-charge were informed and called to the Fortune Gate Security Office, where statements of both the individual and the in-charge were recorded.
RCA-"1.  Unauthorized entry into the scrap yard due to weak access control.
2. Inadequate surveillance and monitoring of the scrap yard area.
3. Lack of regular patrolling and supervision inside the scrap yard
4. High value scrap container was not properly secured
5. Background monitoring of contractor workers was insufficient
6. Suspect had external links with scrap dealers, enabling repeated theft attempts
7. Periodic audits and security checks of the scrap yard were not effectively implemented"

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at IRON AND POWER site at 06 Jan 2026, 1:10 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0110 hours, KEC security guard Vidhya Dhar Bauri (ID: 539553, Mobile No.: 7209592109) reported to the SCCC that five unidentified suspects had entered the Tippler Project area. Upon receipt of the information, the SCCC immediately informed the Security Shift IC, who promptly alerted QRT-01. The QRT team reached the location without delay. However, upon noticing the arrival of the QRT vehicle, all five suspects fled from the area.
The Security Shift IC, along with QRT-02 and the Traffic Team, also reached the location and conducted a thorough search of the area; however, the suspects had already escaped. During further investigation, it was observed that a large quantity of copper cable was present at the site. The suspects had cut the copper cable into five smaller bundles and had attempted to remove them by tying the bundles with their mufflers. While fleeing the scene, the suspects encountered KEC security guard Md. Sabir Ansari (ID: 529678). When the guard raised an alarm, the suspects attempted to assault him. Meanwhile, QRT-01 arrived at the location, prompting the suspects to abandon the copper bundles along the way and flee the area. Despite repeated and intensive search operations carried out by the Security Shift IC, QRT-01, QRT-02, and the Traffic Team, the suspects could not be traced. The five recovered copper bundles, measuring approximately 35–40 meters of copper cable and valued at approximately Rs. 35,000, were secured and transported to the Security Control Room for safekeeping and further necessary action. The incident has been duly intimated to Mr. Sachin Kumar, HR In-Charge, KEC (Mobile No.: 9771784300).

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 08 Jan 2026, 3:20 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 15:20 hrs, QRT-01 informed the Security Shift In-Charge that some unidentified individuals were seen fleeing the area when the QRT team arrived at the location for routine patrolling. Upon verification, it was found that copper cable was being attempted to be stolen from the KEC contractor’s project work area between RMHS MC-1 and MC-2. The Security Shift In-Charge also reached the location where the QRT team was present and conducted a thorough search of the area; however, the suspects had already fled the scene. During further investigation, it was observed that the suspects had cut the copper cable into pieces and concealed them in nearby bushes before fleeing the area upon sighting the QRT team. A total of approximately 40–45 meters of copper cable, valued at around Rs. 40,000, was recovered from the location. At the site, QRT personnel, the Security Shift In-Charge, KEC representatives, and Mr. Saurabh from the Project Department were present. Additionally, KEC has been instructed to install additional lighting at the location to enhance security.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 11 Jun 2025, 7:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 11.06.2025 morning at around 0730hrs security team received a call from the resident of Hati G block regarding missing of his two sport bicycles which was parked near the lift of HG block. During investigations it was noticed that theft of this bicycles happened in intervening night of 10/11 June, 2025 the same has been captured under CCTV footage, it came into observation that three miscreants entered through the route adjacent to the side wall of the building having damaged glass and took 4 bicycle (two sport bicycles as well as two small bicycles of children) in phases between 0024 hrs to 0037 hrs. Later on2 more complaint for missing of bicycles received from the resident of MG and MF and same was captured in CCTV footage of the respective building installed near the lift. The timing of incident was 0137 - 0142 hrs. While investigation on ground it was noticed that those miscreants entered by the boundary wall (by damaging top guard of boundary protections) which is very close to the buildings. Foot marks and scratch were visible observations noticed near and around the peripheral area. Even some of the parts/kit (bag and Locks) belongs to bicycles recovered from the boundary wall area.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at PMP site at 29 Jan 2026, 7:39 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 29 January 2026 at approx. 07:39 hours, during routine frisking at Bravo Foxtrot, contractual worker Arvind Kumar was intercepted by ASO Man Singh while proceeding towards the canteen to bring breakfast.
During the frisking process, HHMD generated a positive alert in the oral region of the individual. Upon further inspection, Arvind Kumar was found to have deliberately concealed a silver button weighing approx170 grams inside his mouth, clearly indicating an attempt to theft. 
When questioned by security personnel, the individual exhibited abnormal behaviour, and lack of clarity in responses. During the interaction, he attempted to remove the concealed silver button and shift it into his pocket, indicating an effort to mislead security Personnel and compromise the frisking process.
Recognizing the suspicious conduct, ASO maintained vigilance. The individual then attempted to move hastily away from the check post, proceeding towards the plant corridor and subsequently the washroom area in a hurry.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 24 Jan 2026, 12:54 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 0054 hrs, a call was received from Rahul Seth reporting suspicious movement of approximately 5–7 individuals near the switchyard watchtower area. Upon receiving the information, the SCR and Baaz teams were immediately alerted and deployed to the location. The teams promptly cordoned off the area and conducted a thorough search operation. During the search, aluminium wire coils weighing approximately 340 kg were found at the site. The impact caused the electric pole to collapse, resulting in a disruption of the power supply to the surrounding area.
The Security Shift In-Charge, QRT, and Electrical Department teams promptly arrived at the site, and the damaged power lines were temporarily shifted to one side of the road to ensure uninterrupted traffic movement. Mr. Ujjwal Choubey, an officer from the Jharkhand Electricity Department, was informed along with the Head of Electrical Department, Mr. Sushil Kumar. After inspection, Mr. Ujjwal Choubey imposed a fine of Rs. 10,000/-, which was deposited by the vehicle owner, following which the matter was resolved. The Jharkhand Electricity Department informed that the electric pole repair work would be completed within 3 to 4 hours. The Security Shift IC, CSR, Traffic Team, and QRT-01 remained present at the site during the incident handling and restoration process.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at IRON AND POWER site at 31 Jan 2026, 8:05 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0805 hrs, the Lady Security Guard deployed at the Oxygen Plant Gate noticed, while conducting routine patrolling, that a group of women from Rajwar Tola had unlawfully entered the Oxygen Plant premises and were engaged in an attempt to steal iron materials.  
Upon noticing the group of women, the Lady Security Guard raised an alarm, causing all of them to flee, abandoning the stolen materials at the spot. She immediately informed the Ansari Gate Commander, Mr. Mithilesh Singh, who arrived promptly; however, the women had already escaped by then. During the subsequent search of the area, all iron materials valued at approximately Rs. 10,000 were recovered. It was also found that the women from Rajwar Tola had gained entry into the Oxygen Plant premises by cutting the fencing wire. This information has additionally been communicated to the Oxygen Plant In-charge.
