INCIDENTTITLE: Chitradurga shift vehicles were intercepted at Jallikatte (Malappanahatti) at 27 Feb 2026, 8:10 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Chitradurga shift vehicles were intercepted at Jallikatte (Malappanahatti) by escort drivers and owners demanding the settlement of pending payments of  October 2025. Although the security team responded immediately by facilitating a joint telephonic discussion with ER/PR, the owners refused to release the vehicles. Consequently, a formal police complaint was lodged by Security via 112 at 0833 hrs, with law enforcement arriving on-site by 0846 hrs. Following further negotiations between the Logistics, Admin, Security, the vehicle owners, and the police, a temporary resolution was reached, and all shift vehicles were successfully released by 0855 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: Gate#3 loaded civil trucks at circle gate were halted at 27 Feb 2026, 9:25 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Gate#3 loaded civil trucks at circle gate were halted by an individual named Nagaraj from Meghalahalli, who demanded the settlement of outstanding payments for dust suppression activity. Upon receiving notice of the disruption, the Security QRT was immediately diverted to the site, and all relevant departments were alerted for corrective action. Following a telephonic intervention by the ER/PR team, the situation was temporarily resolved, allowing gate#3 movement to resume at 0950 hrs while Nagaraj and his family were summoned to the Admin Office for further discussion. However, a secondary disruption occurred at 1035 hrs when Nagaraj’s brother, Suresh, again stopped the loaded trucks with the same demands. After additional negotiations led by ER/PR, the issue was cleared, and all vehicles were released by 1055 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: SOP- LSR Violation at VAL J site at 2026-02-10 16:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1630 hrs. During routine verification of Vehicle Access Passes at the BOP Main Gate, it was observed that the date printed on the Vehicle Access Pass of a Bolero vehicle OD15F1355 driven by Mr. Laxman Dansana (Contractor- KEC, Gate Pass No. 5600002108/10/06705) showed clear signs of manual alteration/tampering. The originally printed date 03.02.2026 appeared to have been overwritten/ modified, indicating unauthorized manipulation of the validity of the vehicle access pass. The vehicle access pass and the driver gate pass were immediately confiscated; the entry of the vehicle was restricted, and the vehicle was held at the BOP Parking Area. A statement was obtained from Mr. Laxman Dansana, and the matter was reported to HR - KEC (Mr. Yadubir) for further necessary action. During the investigation, it was established that Mr. Nakul Naik (Contractor: KEC, Gate Pass No- 5600002108/10/00042), who had also been driving the vehicle, was responsible for the tampering of the Vehicle Access Pass.
RCA-"1. Inadequate awareness of consequences among contractor drivers.
2. Weak monitoring by contractor supervisor regarding pass misuse."

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at VAL J site at 13 Feb 2026, 8:42 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0842 hrs. the following workers of Royal Engineering Company, deployed under Steag Energy Services, were found involved in unauthorized exit activities at the TPP out-punching turnstile gate. Lal Bahadur Yadav (Gate Pass No. 6600016356/20/00395) punched his gate pass and facilitated the exit of Bimlesh Kumar Yadav (Gate Pass No. 6600016356/20/00394) through the turnstile without proper punching. Baban Yadav (Gate Pass No. 6600016356/20/00367) punched his gate pass and allowed Manoj Yadav (Gate Pass No. 6600016356/20/00367) to pass through the turnstile without authorization. Charan Yadav (Gate Pass No. 6600016356/20/00362) punched his gate pass and facilitated the unauthorized exit of Mahendra Yadav through the turnstile. The SCR was informed immediately. The TPP Duty Officer reached the spot promptly and apprehended all the individuals involved. Action Taken- The gate passes of the concerned individuals have been confiscated by the Duty Officer. Self-statements have been obtained from all individuals involved for further necessary action.
RCA-"1. Deliberate violation of access control norms by workers.
2.Weak deterrence perception among contractor workforce.
3.Inadequate supervision at turnstile during peak hours.
4.Lack of strict enforcement culture among contractors.
5.Possible gaps in ACS awareness training."

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 08 Feb 2026, 10:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 22:00 hrs, SCCC observed 03 unidentified individuals entering Garga Yard-2, apparently with the intent to commit theft.
The CRO immediately informed QRT and Security Shift I/C. Upon arrival, they faced stone pelting from the miscreants and took necessary countermeasures. Eventually, the miscreants concealed themselves in nearby bushes and fled the area at around 23:00 hrs. No material loss or injuries were reported.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 10 Feb 2026, 10:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 22:50 hours, the guard on duty at Garga Yard-02 reported to the CCTV operator that he had noticed suspicious or potentially miscreant activity within the yard premises. The CCTV operator immediately scanned the area using available camera feeds; however, no movement or unusual activity was detected. The information was then communicated to the Security Shift IC for further action.
After receiving the information, the Security Shift I/C, along with the QRT team, reached the location and conducted a search of the area at 2344 hrs. During inspection, it was found that an old hole—previously sealed with old CGI sheets had been reopened by miscreants. The security team re-secured the hole, and no material loss was reported from the site.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at DIP site at 11 Feb 2026, 12:20 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 00:20 hours, an estimated 8–10 intruders unlawfully entered the plant premises by cutting the boundary fencing on the Rajwar Tola village side. Their movement was detected in the DIP Finishing Area, where they appeared to be searching for materials. The CCTV operator observed the activity and immediately alerted the stationed security personnel.
Upon receipt of the information, the Security Shift I/C, accompanied by the QRT, promptly responded to the scene. By the time they arrived, the intruders had already fled. A comprehensive search was conducted, and no loss of material has been reported from the area. To ensure strengthened security, an additional guard has been positioned at the site, and the QRT has been instructed to maintain heightened alertness in the zone.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 11 Feb 2026, 1:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 01:30 hours, a group of 3–4 women attempted to breach the premises near the Tower-18 mobile tower by entering through an existing hole in the boundary. The individuals were suspected to be attempting theft. The activity was detected by the CCTV operator, who promptly informed the guard posted in the area.
Upon receipt of the alert, the deployed security guard responded immediately to the location. All individuals attempted to escape and fled upon sighting the guard. A thorough verification revealed no loss of material from the site. The QRT has been instructed to remain on high alert within the area.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at STEEL site at 18 Feb 2026, 10:05 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2205 hrs, SMS Guard Md. Iqbal Ansari informed the QRT and the Security Shift In-Charge that during his routine patrol, he noticed a copper cable lying near the backside drain of the SMS area.
Upon receiving the information, the Security Shift In-Charge along with QRT-2 immediately reached the location and conducted a thorough search of the area. During the operation, approximately 5–6 meters of copper cable, valued at around Rs. 1,000, was recovered from nearby bushes where it had been concealed. No miscreants were found at the site, indicating that they had already fled prior to the team’s arrival. The recovered cable has been securely stored near the control room for further action. Additionally, patrolling frequency in the area has been increased by the security team to prevent recurrence of such incidents. An email has also been communicated to the concerned department, requesting immediate shifting of the cables from the present location to a safer and more secure area.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 27 Feb 2026, 6:03 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: n 27 February 2026, CSOC received intelligence regarding a potential attempt to illicitly remove company materials from the plant premises, following which the Plant 1/3 In-Charge was alerted and security personnel at Parsabhata Gate and Alumina Gate were placed on heightened vigilance. During intensified shift-out frisking, Mr. Dilesh Kumar Bhardwaj (UID: 80000015183) of M/s Larsen & Toubro Limited was intercepted at 18:03 hrs at Alumina Gate with 2.5 kg of copper wire worth Rs. 1,840/- concealed in his bag, and at 18:20 hrs, Mr. Bajrang Das (UID: 1562461) of M/s NTPC GE Power Services was intercepted at Parsabhata Gate with 9 kg of copper wire valued at Rs. 6,624/- hidden in his bag, shoes, and helmet; both abandoned the material at the gates. A subsequent detailed frisking led to the recovery of a total of 11.5 kg of copper wire valued at Rs. 8,464/-, after which their gate passes were confiscated and the seized items secured in the Plant 1 Security Store. Statements from supervisors and concerned personnel were recorded, the matter was escalated to HR for disciplinary action, and both individuals were handed over to the police with formal complaints registered.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at VAL J site at 17 Feb 2026, 6:43 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
There was Intermittent stoppage was reported on 17-02-2026 at 0643 hrs at the Katikela Ash Pond Gate by Bihari Oram along with 6–7 villagers from Katikela, related to an unresolved village welfare fund issue. The matter was informed to the External, CSR, and Ash teams, and normalcy was restored at 0915 hrs, though the issue remained partially resolved. On 18-02-2026 at around 1630 hrs, the same villagers again approached for discussion on the unresolved fund matter and subsequently dug the Katikela road using a JCB. The strike was called off at 2030 hrs, and the road was restored later. On 19-02-2026 at around 10:49 Vishnu Padi and some villagers arrived at pond area and demanding resolution of job related and fund related issue. Situation was immediately escalated and discussed with the CSR & External team for stakeholder engagement and resolution. After discussion they left from location and the situation was normalized at around 14:30 hrs. No operation hamper reported
RCA-Unresolved village welfare fund and job-related commitments, combined with lack of clear communication and closure, triggered repeated agitation by Katikela villagers.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 21 Feb 2026, 11:49 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 11:49 AM, the Power Plant Project In-charge, Mr. Abhishek Kumar, informed the Security Department  that  copper cable had been stolen beside the HCO near the temple during the night of 20.02.2026.
Upon receiving the communication, the security team immediately reached the location and conducted a thorough search of the area. During the search, approximately 10 meters of copper cable were recovered from near the site. An investigation into the theft incident was subsequently initiated.During the investigation, it was found that approximately 30 meters of cable, valued at around ?25,000, were missing. It was also revealed that the site from where the cables were missing was under the responsibility of M/s Onshore, and the cables had been left unsecured and unattended. A formal complaint has been lodged at the local police station regarding the incident. The concerned department and the Onshore team have been instructed to immediately relocate all other valuable items to a secured and monitored area to prevent further incidents.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 12 Mar 2026, 11:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 11:30 hrs, CCTV personnel alerted QRT-4 regarding the unauthorized entry of 4–5 suspected miscreants into Garga Yard-1 with a probable intention of committing theft.
The information was immediately communicated to QRT team and the Security Shift In-Charge, who responded to the location without delay. Upon sighting the security personnel, the miscreants escaped by crossing the yard boundary. Subsequently, they engaged in stone pelting from outside the boundary wall for nearly an hour. The security team advanced with appropriate countermeasures, after which the miscreants fled. No injuries or loss of material were reported.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 21 Mar 2026, 10:17 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Around 22:17 hrs, the CCTV operator noticed five miscreants entering Garga Yard-01 through the boundary wall and informed QRT-04, suspecting theft. 
After receiving the information, the QRT team immediately reached the spot, upon which the miscreants fled temporarily. Shortly thereafter, at around 2300 hrs, they again attempted to enter the yard with intent to commit theft. When QRT approached, the miscreants pelted stones. The Security Shift IC and QRT-02 reached the site for support, and the Siyaljori Police Station was informed. The police patrolling team conducted a joint search with the security team; however, the miscreants escaped. No injuries or material loss were reported.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 16 Mar 2026, 5:55 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:

An immediate response was initiated upon receiving the alert. The deployed security guard and QRT promptly reached the scene, which caused the suspects to flee. During their escape, the suspects abandoned the material at the site, valued at approximately Rs.1,700, thereby successfully preventing the theft.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 27 Feb 2026, 7:48 PM

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at VAL J site at 18 Mar 2026, 8:20 AM

INCIDENTTITLE: PR-Villagers/Neighbourhood at VAL J site at 22 Mar 2026, 1:09 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0109 hrs, a call was received from the security supervisor regarding an attempted theft near Pipeline-01 at Katikela Ash Pond. Miscreants were reported throwing stones from the forest area, hindering apprehension. The incident was informed to CCR at 0111 hrs. As directed by the NDO, teams were mobilized from all sides—Baaz Team from Burkhamunda, Plant-1 Team supported by NDO from the jungle side, and Katikela team from the ash pond pipeline side. A joint search operation was conducted. During inspection, approximately 20 meters was recovered by tracking wire markings. Search operations remain ongoing.
RCA-"1. Remote location near forest provided cover for assailants.
2. Insufficient surveillance and lighting around pipeline area.
3. Limited immediate response capability due to terrain and visibility.
4. Valuable copper wiring left vulnerable without enhanced protection."

INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 30th March 2026, at 1806 hrs. a local individual from Maina village blocked the Maina Junction road using his two-wheeler while in an intoxicated state. The individual began arguing over the absence of the guard. The situation was brought under control and resolved at 1816 hrs. after ASO Sabir Ali discussed the matter with him

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 15 Mar 2026, 4:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
During routine patrolling at around 1630 hrs near the backside of Kurebaga Ash Pond (VMS area backside), the Baaz Team intercepted two individuals attempting to transport SRC company material from the forest side. One individual was carrying approximately 50 kg+ of iron pieces concealed in a cement bag on motorcycle OD-15-K-5720, while another was caught with nearly 10 kg+ of material on a bicycle. Both suspects were detained along with the seized materials for investigation. A third accomplice escaped, and efforts are underway to trace him. All were identified as SRC employees. One employee tested alcohol-positive (120 mg) and was found without PPE. Investigation and actions are ongoing.
RCA-"1.Inadequate surveillance coverage in the backside/forest area.
2.Insufficient patrolling frequency in vulnerable zones.
3.Weak gate pass verification and access control system.
4.Lack of strict monitoring of vendor employees.
5.Poor control over scrap/material handling and disposal process."

INCIDENTTITLE: PR-Villagers/Neighbourhood at VAL J site at 16 Mar 2026, 5:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 0530 hrs, Munna from Banjari village, along with family members and a few villagers, gathered at Banjari Gate and attempted to stop A-shift manpower entry and restrict vehicle movement, citing issues related to employment, stakeholder engagement, village development, and direct contracts. The security team initiated dialogue to de-escalate the situation and promptly informed SCR, CSR, and the External Team. As a precaution, manpower and vehicles were diverted through the Main Gate to ensure uninterrupted operations. Police were informed, and Banjari Gate operations resumed by 1300 hrs, restoring normalcy.
RCA-"1. Employment-related grievances of villagers were not addressed within the expected timeline.
 2. Gaps in stakeholder engagement and communication with the Banjari village representatives.
3. Lack of clarity regarding village development initiatives and direct contract opportunities.
4. Accumulation of unresolved issues resulted in protest at the gate."

2. Employees lacked awareness or disregarded security protocols.
3. Access control system design allowed unauthorized entry by following another user.
4. Absence of immediate deterrent measures (alarms or guards) failed to prevent repeated misuse."

INCIDENTTITLE: Unauthorized entry/ Intrusion/Trespassing at TSPL site at 20 Mar 2026, 9:50 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 21:50 hrs, a call was received from the Railway Building Control Room reporting suspicious activity by unknown miscreants near the railway track and the railway building. Immediately, the Quick Response Team (QRT), along with the intelligence team, rushed to the location. Upon arrival, the area was cordoned off and a thorough search was conducted. However, upon noticing the approaching QRT vehicle, the miscreants fled by jumping over the boundary wall. Footprints indicating the escape route of the miscreants were observed at the site. Subsequently, an ambush was laid in the surrounding area to monitor the situation and prevent any further intrusion.
LNAME: Tower -7, Wagon Tippler Area, TT-5 Area, CHP backside area

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at TSPL site at 30 Mar 2026, 12:35 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"At approximately 00:35 hours, the intelligence team, which had laid an ambush near the DV-93 railway track, reported that four miscreants crossed the track and entered the forest area located in front of the CHP. Simultaneously, a Quick Reaction Team (QRT) positioned itself in ambush near the silo area.
After about one hour, the miscreants attempted to re-emerge from the forest towards the railway track empty-handed. Upon being challenged by the ambush team, they immediately fled back into the forest. The security personnel pursued them; however, the miscreants changed direction and moved towards the silo area, where QRT Team No. 2 was already deployed.
When challenged again by QRT Team No. 2, the miscreants once more retreated into the forest and managed to evade capture. Due to the timely action, alertness, and effective coordination of the deployed security teams, the miscreants failed in their attempt to commit theft."

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 13 Mar 2026, 11:00 PM

The security team responded with suitable countermeasures, after which the miscreants fled. No injuries to personnel or damage/loss of material were reported.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at DIP site at 14 Mar 2026, 11:59 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 23:59 hrs, CCTV surveillance observed and alerted that a group of 5–7 individuals had unlawfully entered near the Dip Finishing area. The individuals were engaged in an attempt to cut cables stored in the DIP store, approaching the location from the boundary wall direction.
Upon receipt of information from CCTV surveillance, the QRT team along with the Security Shift In-charge immediately reached the location. On observing the security personnel, the miscreants escaped from the area, leaving behind approximately 2.5 meters of cable valued Rs. 1000/-, which was recovered by the security team. During a detailed inspection of the area, it was found that the DIP store was not locked, which was under the responsibility of E2E Urja. The concerned supervisor of E2E Urja was called to the site, the store was locked, and instructions were issued to ensure proper locking after work completion.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at ENABLING site at 15 Mar 2026, 6:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At 0600 hrs, family members of deceased employee Vinod Sharma (ID 101358), along with villagers from Modidih and around area, staged a protest near Shikhar Gate, blocking the road with the body brought from Ranchi and demanding compensation. The death was natural, and the employee had been ill and under treatment at Manipal Hospital, Ranchi. Subsequently, road blockades at Bridge, Karma, and Ansari Tola Gates disrupted only vehicular movement, while internal operations continued uninterrupted.
Upon receiving the information, the task force was activated, and details were circulated to all concerned officials. Discussions with the deceased employee’s family representative were held with management. Following successful negotiations, a road blockades were cleared by around 2100 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at VLCTPP site at 2026-03-31 00:14
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 31 March 2026 (C Shift) at approximately 00:14 hrs, Security Guard Kapil Dev, deployed at Watch Tower 10, observed suspicious movement of approximately 1–2 unidentified individuals outside the boundary wall  approaching from the area between Watch Tower 9 and Watch Tower 10, located about 200 meters from the Conveyor Belt CHP. Upon noticing the movement, the guard immediately informed the Main Gate and attempted to pursue the individuals. However, upon sensing the guard’s approach, the unidentified persons fled towards the nearby jungle area, avoiding identification and apprehension. Following the alert, the Gate Supervisor along with the Quick Response Team (QRT) promptly reached the location. A thorough search operation was conducted in the adjacent jungle area.
During the search, the security team recovered two bundles (One Bundles in Night) of electrical cable measuring approximately 100 meters. Additionally, further search operations conducted in the morning led to the recovery of cable (One Bundles in Morning) and one ladder from the incident location.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 18 Mar 2026, 12:15 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0015 hrs, CCTV operators observed a group of 20 to 25 miscreants loitering outside the boundary wall between Tower-17 and Tower-18. Subsequently, a few individuals from the group entered the premises near Tower-17 by breaching the boundary wall with the apparent intention of committing theft.
The CRO promptly informed QRT-1, QRT-2, and the Security Shift IC. Acting on the information, the Security Shift IC and the security team reached the incident location immediately. Upon observing the security personnel, the miscreants initiated stone pelting. The security team successfully controlled the situation through continuous and effective presence, resulting in the miscreants fleeing the area by 0115 hrs.  No material loss or injuries to security staff were reported. Extra guards were deployed to prevent any recurrence.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 18 Mar 2026, 11:35 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 18 March 2026 at approximately 2335 hours, CCTV operators observed a group of miscreants entering the plant premises near the Garga Yard-1 area. They were seen attempting to steal iron material from the location.
The CRO immediately informed the Security Shift I/C and QRT-4. The Security Shift I/C, along with QRT-4 and four guards, promptly reached the location. Upon noticing the approaching security team, the miscreants fled the area, abandoning the material. A thorough search operation was conducted, during which approximately 250 kg of iron material, valued at around Rs 7,000, was recovered and secured by the security team.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at IRON AND POWER site at 20 Mar 2026, 9:15 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2115 hrs, Vinod Mahtha (TPL Supervisor) from PCM informed the CSO that two TPL workers—Subal Chandra Mahtha (ID: 111391) and Rohan Kumbhakar (ID: 83803)—who were on ‘B’ shift duty, were suspected to be under the influence of alcohol.
Following receipt of information, the Security Shift In-charge, accompanied by QRT-2 and the Traffic Team, proceeded to the location to conduct an alcohol test. Both individuals were found to have tested positive. The BAC level recorded was 0.86% for Subal Chandra Mahtha and 0.73% for Rohan Kumbhakar. Written statements were duly recorded. At approximately 2245 hrs, both individuals were punched out and escorted outside the premises.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 20 Mar 2026, 10:18 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Surveillance personnel observed unauthorized entry into the plant premises, and the individuals’ actions were assessed as a potential theft attempt.
Immediate action was taken upon receipt of the alert, and the stationed security guard along with the QRT were promptly mobilized. The suspects were effectively deterred and forced to vacate the site, thereby successfully preventing theft.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 23 Mar 2026, 6:39 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
The control room identified unauthorized persons inside the plant area, and their conduct was assessed as indicative of a possible theft attempt.
An immediate response was initiated upon receiving the alert. The deployed security guard and QRT promptly reached the scene, which caused the suspects to flee. During their escape, the suspects abandoned the material at the site, valued at approximately Rs 2,000, thereby successfully preventing the theft.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 31 Mar 2026, 10:05 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2205 hours, the CCTV operator reported that a group of five individuals had trespassed into Garga Yard-1 with the intent to commit theft.
Upon receiving the information, the Security Shift In-Charge, along with the security team, immediately reached the site. One of the intruders was observed attempting to remove iron materials from the premises but fled upon noticing the presence of security personnel, all miscreants escaped the area. During the operation, approximately 75 kg of scrap material valued at Rs. 5,000 and one hammer weighing about 5 kg were recovered. No loss of material has been reported from the location.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 11 Mar 2026, 7:25 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 1925 hrs, during adverse weather conditions and rainfall, two unknown persons gained unauthorized entry into the Tower-10 project area near the mandir and made an attempt to cut and steal the copper cable kept at the location.
During routine patrolling, Security Guard Parambir Sharma (Emp ID: 9000780) noticed suspicious individuals and promptly informed the QRT team. The QRT team responded immediately and began searching the location. Upon seeing the security personnel, the suspects attempted to escape, leaving behind the cable pieces. During the pursuit, one of the suspects struck the guard with a stick before fleeing. The guard sustained minor injuries and was taken to OHC Hundru, where he received first aid and was released for duty at 2025 hrs. No material loss was reported. Additionally, the involved security personnel have submitted a formal complaint at the local police station regarding the incident.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 24 Mar 2026, 10:25 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 24 March 2026, at around 2225 hours, five unknown individuals unlawfully entered Garga Yard-01 carrying crowbars with the intent to commit theft. The CCTV operator informed the QRT promptly. Upon arrival of the QRT, the suspects spotted the team and fled the location. Subsequently, at approximately 2301 hours, the individuals again entered the yard and attempted to break scrap materials using crowbars.
After receiving the information, the QRT reached the site again and conducted a thorough search. The Security Control Room informed Siyaljori Police Station. During the search, iron rods worth approx. Rs. 1,000, abandoned by the suspects, were recovered. At 2358 hrs, the police team arrived, patrolled the yard, and left. QRT and guards remain alert in the area. No theft or loss of material have been reported from the location.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at IRON AND POWER site at 31 Mar 2026, 2:16 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 1416 hours, the BF-03 Mechanical Head, Mr. Sandeep Singh, informed the Security Department that employee number 101697, Ayodhya Nath Mahto, working as an Operator in BF-03, was appeared to be under the influence of alcohol.
Upon receipt of the information, the Security Team immediately proceeded to the site and conducted an alcohol test, which returned positive with a Blood Alcohol Content (BAC) of 2.22%. During the inquiry, it was revealed that the individual had visited a relative’s residence in Bhagbandh village during lunch hours, where he consumed alcohol. After obtaining a written statement, he was escorted out of the premises by the QRT through Bridge Gate at 1430 hours. The matter has been duly reported to the concerned department.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 11 Feb 2026, 5:48 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 0548 hours, the Security Shift In-Charge and the QRT carried out a surprise inspection near the Rajwar Tola Post. During the search, a few women from Rajwar Tola were intercepted while carrying scrap materials, which were found to be stolen. 
Upon sighting the security team, the women attempted to escape. When the QRT pursued them, they abandoned the scrap materials and fled toward Rajwar Tola village. The QRT recovered all the materials, valued at approximately Rs.1800. Subsequently, a group of women from the village gathered and proceeded toward Ansari Gate. Despite the QRT’s efforts to restrain them, they refused to cooperate and attempted to block the gate. The Security Shift I/C promptly reported the incident to Siyaljori Police Station and informed the relevant officials. At around 0705 hours, the women left from the location on their own and returned to their village.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 12 Feb 2026, 5:47 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 05:47 hrs, the Security Shift I/C and the QRT carried out a surprise inspection in the vicinity of Rajwar Tola Post. During the search, a group of approximately 20–25 women from Rajwar Tola were noticed. They appeared to have covered themselves with sheets, raising suspicion that they might be concealing stolen items.
The Security Shift I/C and QRT stopped the group and instructed them to surrender any items if they had taken anything unlawfully. The women, however, did not cooperate and attempted to exit through the gate. When they were again stopped, they created a disturbance and used the grass-cutting sickles and plastic baskets they were carrying to issue threats. After confirming they had no stolen materials, they were released to return to their villages by 0600 hrs. QRT team has been instructed to remain vigilant at the location.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 23 Feb 2026, 9:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2100 hrs, miscreants intercepted a Gupta Transport vehicle bearing registration number JH09AB 8849, loaded with Pig Iron, near the Garga Weigh Bridge. Around 300 kg of Pig Iron was stolen from the vehicle and dumped outside the boundary wall into the adjoining jungle area. The guard deployed at the location promptly informed the Security Shift I/C and the QRT team.
After being alerted, the Security Shift IC and QRT-4 team immediately arrived at the scene. The miscreants escaped upon sighting the security team. A subsequent search of the area led to the recovery of all stolen Pig Iron (Aprrox. 300 Kg), which had been hidden in the nearby jungle bushes. The vehicle was thereafter safely escorted to the weighbridge, and following due formalities, it was formally handed over to the transporter.

Miscreants started pelting stones upon seeing the security team, but the team acted effectively, forcing them to retreat into nearby bushes. During the search, five scrap motors (approx. 100 kg each) were found kept near the boundary. The Security Shift IC coordinated with Mr. Arjun Shukla to arrange a JCB for their removal and secure storage. The miscreants left the area by 0015 hrs. All items were secured, and no theft or injuries occurred. Additionally, a communication has been sent to all relevant departments instructing that no material be kept near the boundary and that all items must be stored in safe, designated areas.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at STEEL site at 28 Feb 2026, 2:25 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 02:25 hours, the Security Control Room received information regarding suspicious activity involving 4–5 individuals in the WRM Cable Yard, as reported by WRM official Mr. Sudhakar Kumar Singh (Mobile: 8651002309), indicating a possible attempt of theft.
The Control Room Operator immediately informed QRT-2 and the Security Shift IC, following which both promptly reached the location. Upon noticing the arrival of the security team, the suspects attempted to flee. The QRT team pursued them; however, the suspects managed to escape. A thorough search of the area was conducted. During their hurried escape, the suspects left behind two motorcycles (Reg. Nos. JH10AK 7244 and JH09AZ 1318), along with approximately 50 meters of cut copper cable, valued at around Rs. 2,50,000, indicating an attempted theft. Additionally, one bag, an alcohol bottle, and three hacksaw blades with a frame were found abandoned at the site and subsequently recovered. Based on this incident, an FIR was lodged at the Siyaljori Police Station at around 1910 hours by the security team, and all recovered items were formally handed over to the police.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at ENABLING site at 17 Feb 2026, 8:10 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At approximately 2010 hrs, near Tower-09 Onshore Store, the deployed guard observed unidentified miscreants attempting to gain unauthorized entry by cutting one of the iron bars installed over the drain to prevent intrusion. Upon noticing the activity, the guard raised an alarm, following which the miscreants began pelting stones at him
The guard promptly notified the QRT about the incident. Acting on the information, the QRT team quickly arrived at the site. On seeing the QRT, the miscreants continued to throw stones from outside the boundary wall. After some time, they escaped from the area. The QRT has been instructed to remain alert and continues to maintain vigilant monitoring in the vicinity. No material loss or injury have been reported from the location.

INCIDENTTITLE: Fraud/Malpractice at RDC site at 04 Mar 2026, 9:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 04.03.2026, at around 09:30 hrs, at DSC near the RD parking side on the VTC Road, upon receiving information by the intelligence team member PL Dhaka, the QRT and Security Officer Navaratan Sharma immediately reached the location. The driver of Bharat Construction Company, Mr. Ramesh Bhil (Driving License No. RJ3020180004141), who was driving Dumper Vehicle No. RJ30GA5868 was found involved in illegal diesel trade with Mr. Dinesh Jatiya. Both individuals were caught red-handed while illegally selling approximately 30 liters of diesel. The diesel was taken from Dumper No. RJ30GA5868. Driver Mr. Ramesh Bhil has been working with Bharat Construction Company for the past 8 years in the common area of RD/DSC. During discussion and preliminary inquiry, the suspects confessed to previous instances of diesel theft. Mr. Ramesh Bhil admitted that while performing trips for DSC/RD, he had engaged in illegal diesel trade on multiple occasions in the past. Formal statements from both suspects were recorded. The driver has been blocked in SAP. Both individuals have been handed over to the Dariba Police Chauki.                                                                                                                                                       The total recover material value is approximately Rs. 2500.
